articles.title: Photos: Messi’s Argentina stun England to reach World Cup final vs Spain
articles.summary: Messi inspires Argentina’s dramatic late comeback win over England to reach final against Spain.
articles.titleZh: 照片：梅西的阿根廷队击败英格兰队进入世界杯决赛，对阵西班牙队
articles.summaryZh: 梅西激励阿根廷队在最后时刻戏剧性地逆转战胜英格兰队，进入决赛对阵西班牙队。

contentParagraphs.en: Messi inspires Argentina’s late comeback against England to reach World Cup final vs Spain.Lionel Messi celebrates with his teammates after Argentina's semifinal victory over England [Alex Livesey/Danehouse/Getty Images]Published On 16 Jul 2026Lautaro Martinez scored a 92nd-minute winner as Lionel Messi inspired World Cup holders Argentina to a stunning 2-1 comeback victory over England, setting up a final against European champions Spain.England had been on course to reach their first World Cup final since 1966 after Anthony Gordon fired them ahead 10 minutes into the second half of Wednesday’s semifinal, played in front of 68,239 fans in Atlanta.The fierce rivalry between these nations has produced several memorable contests on the World Cup stage over the years, and this encounter will be remembered in Argentina as the stuff of legend after the South Americans denied England with two late goals.Messi set up Enzo Fernandez to drill in an 85th-minute equaliser and then, with extra time looming, crossed for substitute Lautaro Martinez to head in the winner in the second minute of stoppage time.No team has retained the World Cup since Brazil in 1962, and now Messi will become only the second player, after Brazilian great Cafu, to appear in three World Cup finals.The final will be played at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey on Sunday, as the first 48-team World Cup culminates in a showdown between the reigning champions of Europe and South America.Argentina's Lautaro Martinez scores their second goal during the World Cup semifinal match between England and Argentina in Atlanta. [Erik S. Lesser/AP Photo]Lionel Messi inspired World Cup holders Argentina to a stunning comeback to beat England 2-1. [Brett Davis/IMAGN IMAGES via Reuters]Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham at the end of the match. [Jeff Roberson/AP Photo]England supporters watch the match between England and Argentina, at Boxpark Wembley in London. [Brook Mitchell/AFP]Fans celebrates in front of a mural of Lionel Messi after Argentina defeated England, in Buenos Aires. [Gustavo Garello/AP Photo]Fans celebrate Argentina's win at a watch party in Kansas City. [Charlie Riedel/AP Photo]Fans celebrate Argentina's victory over England, in Buenos Aires. [Rodrigo Abd/AP Photo]Messi and his teammates celebrate after their win. [Jacob Kupferman/AP Photo]Argentina players celebrate in front of their supporters. [Erik S. Lesser/AP Photo]
contentParagraphs.zh: 梅西激励阿根廷队在对阵英格兰队的比赛中逆转晋级对阵西班牙队的世界杯决赛。阿根廷半决赛战胜英格兰队后，莱昂内尔·梅西与队友们庆祝 [Alex Livesey/Danehouse/Getty Images]发布于 2026 年 7 月 16 日，劳塔罗·马丁内斯在第 92 分钟攻入制胜球，莱昂内尔·梅西激励世界杯卫冕冠军阿根廷队以 2-1 逆转战胜英格兰队，为决赛对阵欧洲冠军西班牙队奠定了基础。英格兰队一直在走上正轨。星期三半决赛在亚特兰大举行，在 68,239 名球迷面前，安东尼·戈登 (Anthony Gordon) 在 68,239 名球迷面前进行了比赛，在安东尼·戈登 (Anthony Gordon) 的帮助下，他们自 1966 年以来首次进入世界杯决赛。这些年来，这些国家之间的激烈竞争在世界杯舞台上产生了几场令人难忘的比赛，在南美队在最后时刻以两个进球否定了英格兰队之后，这场比赛将在阿根廷被铭记为传奇。梅西安排恩佐·费尔南德斯 (Enzo Fernandez) 训练第 85 分钟扳平比分，然后在加时赛临近时，补时第 2 分钟传中，替补出场的劳塔罗·马丁内斯头球破门。自 1962 年巴西队以来，没有哪支球队卫冕过世界杯，而现在，梅西将成为继巴西伟大的卡福之后，第二位三度闯入世界杯决赛圈的球员。决赛将于周日在新泽西州大都会人寿体育场举行，第一届 48 队世界杯将在欧洲和南美洲的卫冕冠军。阿根廷队的劳塔罗·马丁内斯在亚特兰大举行的世界杯半决赛英格兰队与阿根廷队的比赛中打进了他们的第二个进球。 [Erik S. Lesser/美联社照片]莱昂内尔·梅西 (Lionel Messi) 激励世界杯卫冕冠军阿根廷队以惊人的逆转以 2-1 击败英格兰队。 [Brett Davis/IMAGN IMAGES viaReuters]哈里·凯恩和裘德·贝林厄姆在比赛结束时。 [Jeff Roberson/美联社照片]英格兰支持者在伦敦温布利 Boxpark 观看英格兰和阿根廷之间的比赛。 [布鲁克·米切尔/法新社]在布宜诺斯艾利斯，阿根廷队击败英格兰队后，球迷们在梅西的壁画前庆祝。 [古斯塔沃·加雷洛/美联社照片]球迷在堪萨斯城的观看派对上庆祝阿根廷队的胜利。 [Charlie Riedel/美联社照片]球迷在布宜诺斯艾利斯庆祝阿根廷战胜英格兰。 [罗德里戈·阿卜杜勒/美联社照片]梅西和他的队友在获胜后庆祝。 [Jacob Kupferman/美联社照片]阿根廷球员在支持者面前庆祝。 [埃里克·S·莱瑟/美联社照片]

articles.title: UK investigation to determine if TikTok fails to protect children from harmful content
articles.summary:
Ofcom concerned platform’s age verification is ineffective, leaving some at risk of seeing posts about self-harm and suicide
TikTok is under formal investigation over concerns it has failed to protect children from harmful content, the UK’s online regulator, Ofcom, has announced.
articles.titleZh: 英国调查确定 TikTok 是否未能保护儿童免受有害内容的侵害
articles.summaryZh:
Ofcom相关平台的年龄验证无效，导致一些人面临看到有关自残和自杀的帖子的风险
英国网络监管机构 Ofcom 宣布，由于担心 TikTok 未能保护儿童免受有害内容的侵害，TikTok 正在接受正式调查。

contentParagraphs.en: TikTok is under formal investigation over concerns it has failed to protect children from harmful content, the UK’s online regulator, Ofcom, has announced.The social media platform’s approach to checking the ages of users has sparked “particular concerns” at the watchdog, almost a year after measures to protect children from the worst of online content came into effect under the Online Safety Act.Ofcom said TikTok is using a method of inferring children’s ages that may have failed to correctly identify “a significant proportion of children”, putting them at risk of exposure to harmful content.The regulator said it had not reached any conclusions, but that compliance failures could be punished with fines of up to £18m or 10% of qualifying worldwide revenue, whichever is greater. Harmful content includes posts about disordered eating, self-harm, suicide and pornography. Ofcom can also apply to have sites blocked or restricted in the UK in the most serious cases.Ofcom said: “This investigation will seek to establish whether there are reasonable grounds to believe that TikTok has failed, or is failing, to comply with its legal obligations … including by using age assurance that is highly effective at correctly determining whether or not a particular user is a child.”TikTok says that it requires users to enter a date of birth when they create an account. It says “we also use technology that looks at information, often called ‘signals’, to check for indicators that someone may not meet our minimum age requirement.”The investigation comes as the UK government prepares to launch a social media ban for under-16s early next year, which will increase scrutiny of the methods tech companies use for verifying users ages.Keir Starmer announces social media ban for under-16s in UK – videoOfcom said it also had “serious doubts” about other platforms using techniques that infer users’ ages. It said that “in some cases, tech companies may be failing to correctly detect significant numbers of children on their platforms, meaning children risk being exposed to harmful content”.It said “those which use age inference models to comply with their child protection duties should switch to other methods listed in our guidance as highly effective without delay”.For example, Ofcom’s research found that about one in 10 teenagers aged between 15 and 17 were still using the three most-used dating apps in December 2025, despite age checks being in place.TikTok is the third most used site or app by 8- to 14-year-olds, after YouTube and WhatsApp, according to Ofcom’s studies, with children spending an average of eight hours 45 minutes a week on video-sharing platforms, including TikTok, YouTube, Twitch and DailyMotion.In a statement, it said: “We strictly enforce age-appropriate experiences through expert-informed platform rules and ​advanced age inference technologies, in line with ​major ⁠industry peers. We are confident that we meet ⁠our ​Online Safety Act obligations and ​will work with Ofcom to demonstrate this.”It also says it does not allow content that promotes disordered eating or shows risky weight management behaviours.Meanwhile, Ofcom has also warned that children are too easily finding links to pornography sites with no age checks by using search engines. It has found that one in three results returned on the first page of Google Search and 54% on Microsoft-owned Bing sent users to such check-free sites.The regulator said about a quarter of the UK’s most popular pornography services in the UK had no checks in place. Since 25 July last year, all sites and apps in the UK which allow pornography have been required under the Online Safety Act to have age checks in place to protect children from accessing harmful content.Ofcom said the two search engines will now be working with the regulator to tackle the discoverability of pornography sites which still do not have age checks.Microsoft declined to comment. Google has been approached for comment.
contentParagraphs.zh: 英国网络监管机构 Ofcom 宣布，由于担心 TikTok 未能保护儿童免受有害内容的侵害，TikTok 正在接受正式调查。该社交媒体平台检查用户年龄的方法引发了监管机构的“特别关注”，距离保护儿童免受最恶劣在线内容侵害的措施根据《网络安全法》生效已近一年。Ofcom 表示，TikTok 正在使用一种推断儿童年龄的方法，该方法可能无法正确识别“很大一部分儿童”，使他们面临被攻击的风险。监管机构表示尚未得出任何结论，但如果不合规，可能会被处以最高 1800 万英镑的罚款或合格全球收入的 10%，以较高者为准。有害内容包括有关饮食失调、自残、自杀和色情内容的帖子。在最严重的情况下，Ofcom 还可以申请屏蔽或限制英国境内的网站。 Ofcom 表示：“这项调查将寻求确定是否有合理的理由相信 TikTok 未能或正在未能遵守其法律义务……包括使用年龄保证，该保证在正确确定特定用户是否为儿童方面非常有效。”TikTok 表示，它要求用户在创建帐户时输入出生日期。它说：“我们还使用查看信息的技术（通常称为‘信号’）来检查某人可能不符合我们最低年龄要求的指标。”这项调查正值英国政府准备明年初针对 16 岁以下青少年推出社交媒体禁令，这将加强对科技公司用于验证用户年龄的方法的审查。Keir Starmer 宣布英国针对 16 岁以下青少年的社交媒体禁令 - videoOfcom 表示，它也对其他使用以下技术的平台表示“严重怀疑”：推断用户的年龄。报告称，“在某些情况下，科技公司可能无法正确检测其平台上的大量儿童，这意味着儿童面临接触有害内容的风险”。报告称，“那些使用年龄推断模型来履行儿童保护义务的人应立即改用我们指南中列出的其他非常有效的方法。”例如，Ofcom 的研究发现，尽管进行了年龄检查，但 2025 年 12 月，大约十分之一的 15 至 17 岁青少年仍在使用三种最常用的约会应用程序根据 Ofcom 的研究，TikTok 是 8 至 14 岁儿童使用次数第三多的网站或应用程序，仅次于 YouTube 和 WhatsApp，儿童平均每周在视频共享平台上花费 8 小时 45 分钟，包括 TikTok、YouTube、Twitch 和 DailyMotion。 ”业界同行。我们有信心履行我们的《在线安全法案》义务，并将与 Ofcom 合作证明这一点。”该法案还表示，它不允许宣传饮食失调或表现出危险的体重管理行为的内容。同时，Ofcom 还警告说，儿童很容易通过搜索引擎找到没有年龄检查的色情网站链接。调查发现，谷歌搜索首页上返回的结果有三分之一，微软旗下必应搜索结果中有 54% 的结果将用户引导至此类免检查网站。该监管机构表示，英国最受欢迎的色情服务中约有四分之一没有进行检查。自去年 7 月 25 日起，英国所有允许色情内容的网站和应用程序都必须根据《在线安全法》进行年龄检查，以保护儿童免受有害内容的侵害。Ofcom 表示，这两个搜索引擎现在将与监管机构合作，解决仍然没有年龄检查的色情网站的可发现性问题。微软拒绝发表评论。已联系谷歌征求意见。

articles.title: UK economy returns to growth in May
articles.summary: The modest growth reverses a slight contraction that had been seen the previous month.
articles.titleZh: 英国经济五月恢复增长
articles.summaryZh: 温和的增长扭转了上个月出现的小幅收缩。

contentParagraphs.en: The UK's economy returned to growth in May, but the expansion was modest as businesses were affected by the impact of the Iran war.The economy grew by 0.1%, the Office for National Statistics (ONS) said, driven by expansion in the UK's service sector, although this was offset by falls in the production and construction sectors.May's growth comes after a slight contraction in April, and analysts said the latest figures suggested the economy had weathered the rise in energy prices caused by the conflict in the Middle East better than expected.However, others noted the UK economy remained "fragile" and incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham faced a challenge to boost growth.Over the three months to May, the ONS said the economy grew by 0.7%, external compared with the previous three-month period."The economy recorded robust growth in the three months to May, though the pace eased slightly as the latest two months showed a weaker picture," said Liz McKeown, director of economic statistics at the ONS."Computer programming and advertising led the way, while the often-volatile pharmaceutical industry also performed well," she added.The economy saw a strong start to the year, but growth has faltered in recent months with the conflict in the Middle East having affected some businesses.The Iran war has pushed up oil and fuel prices, and also disrupted supply chains. The ONS said firms in a number of sectors had flagged the conflict as affecting activity, including some manufacturing industries, hospitality firms, travel agencies and entertainment companies.Since hostilities resumed between the US and Iran last week, the price of oil has risen from about $72 a barrel to $84, although it remains well below the peak of around $120 seen earlier this year."Today's data confirm that growth remains fragile," said Fergus Jimenez-England, associate economist at the National Institute of Economic and Social Research."As energy prices climb once more, all eyes are now on the new prime minister to deliver much-needed stability."Yael Selfin, chief economist at KPMG, said the warmer weather and World Cup might have boosted consumer spending into June and July, but this "may not be enough to offset weakness across other parts of the economy"."The recent rise in energy prices, driven by a pick-up in tensions in the Middle East, could pose a risk to the growth outlook, with financial conditions also tightening as a result," Selfin said.The growth in May "is not a bad welcome gift for incoming PM Andy Burnham", said Paul Dales, chief UK economist at Capital Economics."But with higher energy prices still restraining real incomes, he shouldn't get used to it."Responding to the latest figures, a spokesperson for the Treasury said: "We have the right economic plan which has put the UK in a much stronger position than two years ago with the fastest growth in the G7 in the first quarter and the OECD agreeing that we have restored stability."But Conservative shadow chancellor Sir Mel Stride said chancellor Rachel Reeves had "failed" in her attempts to boost growth."Two years of higher taxes have choked the economy, and now Andy Burnham wants even more taxes to pay for more benefits. "Starmer and Reeves may be on their way out but the problem isn't just the chancellor, it's the Labour Party."
contentParagraphs.zh: 英国经济5月份恢复增长，但由于企业受到伊朗战争的影响，扩张幅度不大。英国国家统计局(ONS)表示，在英国服务业扩张的推动下，英国经济增长了0.1%，尽管这被生产和建筑业的下滑所抵消。5月份的增长是在4月份小幅收缩之后出现的，分析师表示，最新数据表明，英国经济比预期更好地抵御了中东冲突导致的能源价格上涨。其他人指出，英国经济仍然“脆弱”，即将上任的首相安迪·伯纳姆面临提振增长的挑战。英国国家统计局表示，截至 5 月份的三个月内，与前三个月相比，外部经济增长了 0.7%。英国国家统计局经济统计主任利兹·麦基翁 (Liz McKeown) 表示，“截至 5 月份的三个月内，经济录得强劲增长，但由于最近两个月的情况较疲弱，经济增速略有放缓。”经常波动的制药行业也表现良好。”她补充道。今年经济开局强劲，但近几个月增长放缓，中东冲突影响了一些企业。伊朗战争推高了石油和燃料价格，也扰乱了供应链。英国国家统计局表示，包括一些制造业、酒店业、旅行社和娱乐公司在内的多个行业的公司都已将冲突标记为影响经济活动。自上周美国和伊朗之间恢复敌对行动以来，石油价格已从每桶约 72 美元上涨至 84 美元，但仍远低于今年早些时候约 120 美元的峰值。“今天的数据证实增长仍然脆弱，”国家经济研究所副经济学家弗格斯·希门尼斯-英格兰表示。毕马威(KPMG)首席经济学家耶尔·塞尔芬(Yael Selfin)表示，气候变暖和世界杯可能会提振6月和7月的消费者支出，但这“可能不足以抵消经济其他领域的疲软”。“随着能源价格再次攀升，所有人的目光都集中在新总理带来急需的稳定上。”近期，受中东紧张局势升级推动的能源价格上涨，可能对增长前景构成风险，金融状况也在收紧。凯投宏观(Capital Economics)首席英国经济学家保罗·戴尔斯(Paul Dales)表示，5月份的经济增长“对即将上任的首相安迪·伯纳姆来说是一个不错的欢迎礼物”。“但由于能源价格上涨仍然限制了实际收入，他不应该习惯这种情况。”针对最新数据，财政部发言人表示：“我们制定了正确的经济计划，使英国处于比两年前更有利的地位，第一季度是七国集团和经合组织中增长最快的国家。”同意我们已经恢复了稳定。”但保守党影子财政大臣梅尔·斯特莱德表示，财政大臣雷切尔·里夫斯提振增长的努力“失败”了。“两年来的高税收已经抑制了经济，现在安迪·伯纳姆希望用更多的税来支付更多的福利。斯塔默和里夫斯可能即将下台，但问题不仅仅是财政大臣，而是工党。”

articles.title: New monkey species with orange lips found 'hiding' in DR Congo forest
articles.summary: Scientists describe "amazing feeling" to look into the face of an animal that so few people knew existed.
articles.titleZh: 刚果民主共和国森林中发现“隐藏”的橙色嘴唇新猴种
articles.summaryZh: 科学家们形容，看到一种很少有人知道其存在的动物的脸时会有“奇妙的感觉”。

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, Junior AmbokoImage caption, The monkey, known locally as 'Likweli', has distinctive pinkish-orange lipsByVictoria GillScience correspondent A monkey that has striking pinkish-orange lips and a black face - and lives in the forests of the Democratic Republic of Congo - has been confirmed as a new species to science.The black-furred primate was spotted and photographed hidden away in the high tree canopy of dense tropical forests in Lomami National Park, in the central east of the country.Conservationists working there first reported seeing this unusual-looking animal back in 2008. But they captured just one blurry photograph.After another sighting 10 years later, an international team set out to find and study the monkey and revealed that it was a previously unknown species.This is only the fifth African monkey species to be discovered in the last 75 years. Junior Amboko, a PhD student at Florida Atlantic University, played a leading role in the search, which involved audio recordings, photography and detailed genetic studies.The findings were published in the journal PLoS One, external.Amboko told BBC News it was an "amazing feeling" to look into the face of an animal that so few people knew existed.Secretive speciesImage source, Junior AmbokoImage caption, Junior Amboko (left) and Mardoché B. Koko recorded the distinctive, resonant roar of the newly discovered Colobus congoensis monkey in the Lomami National Park, DRC"Discovering" a species, in this context, means officially recording and confirming it has evolved to be genetically distinct. Some local people already knew of the monkey's existence and refer to it by a common name - Likweli.But Amboko said the monkeys were "kind of shy" - and tended to hide high in the trees."As part of our search, we interviewed people in 52 villages close to where the animals live. And only people in eight villages [had ever seen] them," he recalled.The research team - from DRC, the US and Germany - gave the animal the Latin name Colobus congoensis in recognition of the natural diversity in the country.It belongs to the larger group of colobus monkeys. "These are really important African monkeys that don't have thumbs," explained Prof Kate Detwiler from Florida Atlantic University.Figure caption, The Likweli monkey's distinctive roaring call"They're these herbivores of the canopy that are a critical part of the ecosystem. We think they have a lot to do with processing seeds and germination in the forest."Prof Detwiler speculates that their unusual, bright facial markings could be a visual signal to other animals, potentially attractive to a mate or that it could allow the animals to identify each other.The monkeys also have a distinctive "roaring" call. "You often hear them, but don't see them," said Amboko.They think the animals are rare and restricted to a part of the forest where they can find the food and habitat they need.The animals are hunted for meat, so the researchers hope that now that Colobus congoensis is classified as a distinct species it can be officially protected.The researchers say they still have a lot of questions about this newly described, secretive species. They plan to carry out a more detailed survey to estimate their population and study their behaviour.

articles.title: Decades of denial: Death certificate for disappeared reopens Kashmir wounds
articles.summary: After years of searching and court battles, a judge declares what Junaid Rashid already believed: His father was dead.
articles.titleZh: 数十年的否认：失踪者的死亡证明重新揭开了克什米尔的伤口
articles.summaryZh: 经过多年的搜寻和法庭斗争，法官宣布了朱奈德·拉希德已经相信的事情：他的父亲已经死了。

contentParagraphs.en: Junaid Rashid was five when his father vanished from military custody nearly three decades ago, one of the thousands who disappeared during the armed rebellion in India-administered Kashmir.But after years of the family searching for him and fighting court battles, a judge in the contested Himalayan territory declared in April what Rashid already believed: His father, Abdul Rashid Wani, was dead.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Wave of Kashmir disappearances, mystery deaths spook tribal communitylist 2 of 4Kashmiri rights activist wins partial court victory but remains behind barslist 3 of 4Two Kashmir brothers: One killed by rebels, another by army 26 years laterlist 4 of 4‘Limited damage’: Upcoming Bollywood film angers Kashmir pellet gun victimsend of listIt was the first such ruling among thousands of petitions for the disappeared, marking a rare recognition that many other families still don’t have of their loss.The judgement ordered the issuance of a “death certificate” and also acknowledged a police investigation that identified the army officer who took Wani into custody in July 1997.Army major ‘murdered Wani’Wani, a timber trader, was stopped near his home in the city of Srinagar while carrying “a good amount of cash” on his way to pay suppliers, according to his family and the police investigation.That evening, his wife and two children sat “all dressed up”, waiting for him to return to take them to a wedding reception.“He never came back,” Rashid told the AFP news agency.The ruling, citing the inquiry, said the accused, an army major, “had murdered Abdul Rashid Wani in his custody and had disposed of his corpse”.It records the date of Wani’s death as the same day that he vanished but gives no information as to where his body lies.“The government has now, after 29 years, acknowledged in court that such an atrocity was done,” said Rashid, now 34.In Kashmir, the wives of the missing men are known as “half-widows” – unable to mourn fully until they know their husbands are dead.“If this had happened earlier, I think Kashmir would look different,” Rashid added. “Our lives would look different, and my mother’s health would be something else.”Muslim-majority Kashmir has been divided between India and Pakistan since their independence from British rule in 1947. Both claim the Himalayan territory in full, and the nuclear-armed neighbours have fought multiple conflicts since – the most recent last year.In 1989, after failed political struggles for the right of self-determination, rebel groups began an armed struggle. They sought Kashmir’s independence or its merger with Pakistan.New Delhi deployed its soldiers, accusing Pakistan of backing the rebels – an allegation Islamabad denies.The scenic tourist destination was transformed into one of the most militarised spots in the world. Tens of thousands of people, mostly civilians, have been killed since.Today, the rebellion has been largely crushed, but at least 500,000 Indian soldiers remain stationed there.Rights groups say 8,000 missingThe People’s Union for Democratic Rights, a civil liberties group based in New Delhi, said Wani’s judicial declaration of death “encapsulates the human rights story” in Kashmir since violence surged in 1989.It said Wani was just one case among many of “enforced disappearance”.There could be as many as 8,000 disappeared people, according to the rights group Association of Parents of Disappeared Persons (APDP). Some of them, it said, were likely abducted by rebels.It mapped in 2009 what it said were 2,700 unmarked graves in remote mountain zones along the de facto border with Pakistan. It also quoted residents alleging they had buried mutilated bodies left by security forces.Among those sites was Kupwara, where residents showed AFP rows of graves marked by rusting metal numbered signs.A woman brings flowers to a graveyard in Sonamarg, a remote mountainous resort in Indian-administered Kashmir, on November 3, 2020 [Aijaz Hussain/AP]One man in his mid-40s said that from 1990 to 2000, he and the villagers buried an estimated 500 bodies left by the police as “humanitarian work”. The police left the bodies, without saying who they were, he said.“Later, we opened graves for relatives of missing Kashmiris,” he said, adding that some families were able to identify the bodies.New Delhi and security authorities insisted the bodies were of fighters killed in clashes who they could not identify. They said the missing men were likely to have crossed into Pakistan.Kashmir’s State Human Rights Commission also examined the graves. In 2011, it found bodies buried at 38 locations identified by the APDP and said the government had identities for only 464 of the 2,730 bodies at the sites.The commission said it was possible that “many disappeared persons” may be found in the unmarked graves.DNA testing it has called for has not been carried out, and the commission was shut down in 2019 after New Delhi’s central government took direct control of Kashmir.‘Midnight knock’Rashid said his family had “spared no effort” to find Wani, including selling their family home to raise funds.They faced pressure to stop, saying they were offered cash from army officers to abandon their search after being told privately by them that “what has happened has happened”, Rashid said.“I remember my grandmother telling a colonel at our home, ‘Just give me my son back,'” Rashid said.They were also asked to pay for help to secure Wani’s release by a group of former rebels that has since surrendered and sided with the government. Instead, the family pursued the case in court.Rashid, who visited an army camp with his mother searching for Wani, said he met the officer whom the police investigation named as ordering Wani’s detention. “I was very young, but I still remember his face,” Rashid said.A protest in Kashmir demands the return of bodies of civilians for proper burial in Srinagar on November 18, 2021 [Mukhtar Khan/AP]Wani’s case is just on
contentParagraphs.zh: 近三十年前，朱奈德·拉希德 (Junaid Rashid) 五岁时，他的父亲从军方拘留所失踪，他是印控克什米尔武装叛乱期间失踪的数千人之一。但经过家人多年寻找他并在法庭上进行斗争后，有争议的喜马拉雅地区的一名法官于 4 月宣布了拉希德已经相信的事情：他的父亲阿卜杜勒·拉希德·瓦尼 (Abdul Rashid Wani) 已经死亡。推荐故事列表 4 条新闻列表 4 波克什米尔失踪、神秘死亡中的第 1 条惊悚部落社区成员 4 中的 2 克什米尔权利活动家赢得了部分法院胜利，但仍然落后于律师名单 4 中的 3 两兄弟：一个被叛乱分子杀害，另一个在 26 年后被军队杀害 列出 4 中的 4 “有限的损害”：即将上映的宝莱坞电影激怒了克什米尔弹丸枪受害者名单结束这是数千份失踪人员请愿书中的第一个此类裁决，标志着许多其他家庭仍然没有意识到他们的损失。该判决命令签发了“死亡证明”，并承认警方调查确定了 1997 年 7 月拘留 Wani 的军官。根据他的家人和警方的调查，陆军少校“谋杀了木材商 Wani'Wani”，在前往斯利那加市的家附近被拦住，当时他携带“大量现金”去向供应商付款。那天晚上，他的妻子和两个孩子“盛装打扮”，等待他回来带他们去参加婚宴。拉希德告诉法新社。裁决援引调查结果称，被告是一名陆军少校，“在他的监护下谋杀了阿卜杜勒·拉希德·瓦尼，并处理了他的尸体”。裁决将瓦尼的死亡日期记录为他失踪的同一天，但没有提供他的尸体埋在哪里的信息。“29年后，政府现在在法庭上承认发生了这样的暴行，”拉希德说，现在34.在克什米尔，失踪男子的妻子被称为“半寡妇”——在知道丈夫去世之前无法完全哀悼。“如果这件事早点发生，我认为克什米尔看起来会有所不同，”拉希德补充道。 “我们的生活会有所不同，我母亲的健康状况也会有所不同。” 自 1947 年脱离英国统治独立以来，穆斯林占多数的克什米尔一直被印度和巴基斯坦瓜分。两国都声称拥有喜马拉雅山全部领土，自那以来，这两个拥有核武器的邻国一直在发生多起冲突——最近一次是去年。1989 年，在争取自决权的政治斗争失败后，叛乱团体开始了武装斗争。他们寻求克什米尔独立或与巴基斯坦合并。新德里部署了士兵，指责巴基斯坦支持叛乱分子——伊斯兰堡否认了这一指控。这个风景秀丽的旅游胜地变成了世界上军事化程度最高的景点之一。此后，已有数万人被杀，其中大部分是平民。如今，叛乱已基本被镇压，但至少有 50 万印度士兵仍驻扎在那里。人权组织称有 8,000 人失踪。总部位于新德里的公民自由组织民主权利人民联盟表示，瓦尼的司法宣告死亡“概括了自 1989 年暴力事件激增以来克什米尔的人权故事”。这只是众多“强迫失踪”案件中的一个。据权利组织失踪者家长协会（APDP）称，失踪者可能多达 8000 人。据称，其中一些人可能被叛军绑架。2009年，该公司绘制了地图，据称在与巴基斯坦事实上的边界沿线的偏远山区有2,700个没有标记的坟墓。它还引用了居民的说法，称他们埋葬了安全部队留下的残缺不全的尸体。其中包括库普瓦拉，那里的居民向法新社展示了成排的坟墓，上面刻着生锈的金属编号标志。2020 年 11 月 3 日，一名妇女将鲜花带到印控克什米尔偏远山区度假胜地索纳玛格的墓地 [Aijaz Hussain/美联社]一名 40 多岁的男子说，从 1990 年到2000年，他和村民埋葬了警察留下的约500具尸体，作为“人道主义工作”。他说，警方留下了尸体，但没有透露他们是谁。“后来，我们为失踪的克什米尔人的亲属打开了坟墓，”他说，并补充说，一些家庭能够辨认尸体。新德里和安全当局坚称，这些尸体是在冲突中丧生的战士的尸体，但他们无法辨认身份。他们说失踪人员很可能已经越境进入巴基斯坦。克什米尔国家人权委员会也检查了这些坟墓。 2011年，它在APDP确定的38个地点发现了埋藏的尸体，并表示政府只掌握了这些地点2,730具尸体中464具的身份。该委员会表示，在没有标记的坟墓中可能会发现“许多失踪者”。它所要求的DNA测试尚未进行，并且在新德里中央政府直接控制克什米尔后，该委员会于2019年被关闭。拉希德说，他的家人“不遗余力”寻找瓦尼，包括卖掉家里的房子来筹集资金。他们面临着停止的压力，称他们在被军官私下告知“发生的事情已经发生了”后，向他们提供了现金，要求他们放弃搜寻。拉希德说，“我记得我的祖母告诉我们家的一位上校，‘把我的儿子还给我’。”拉希德说。他们还被一群前警察要求支付帮助，以确保瓦尼获释。叛乱分子后来投降并站在政府一边。相反，家人在法庭上追查此案。 拉希德与母亲一起前往军营寻找瓦尼，他说他遇到了警方调查中下令拘留瓦尼的警官。拉希德说：“我当时很年轻，但我仍然记得他的脸。”克什米尔的一场抗议活动要求归还平民尸体，以便于 2021 年 11 月 18 日在斯利那加妥善安葬 [Mukhtar Khan/美联社]瓦尼的案件刚刚开始

articles.title: TikTok faces Ofcom investigation over child age checks
articles.summary: It follows a review by the regulator in May that criticised the platform for not being "safe enough" for children.
articles.titleZh: TikTok 因儿童年龄检查面临 Ofcom 调查
articles.summaryZh: 此前，监管机构在五月份进行了一项审查，批评该平台对儿童来说“不够安全”。

contentParagraphs.en: An investigation has been launched into whether TikTok is doing enough to keep children off its platform.The probe by media regulator Ofcom comes a month after the UK government announced that under-16's would be banned entirely from a range of platforms.Ofcom will examine how the video-sharing app assesses if a user is a child and whether it has adequate systems to prevent children from viewing harmful content."We're confident that we meet our Online Safety Act obligations and will work with Ofcom to demonstrate it," a TikTok spokesperson said.It follows a review by regulator in May which criticised the platform for not being "safe enough" for children and called for stronger action on children's online safety.Kate Davies, Ofcom's group director for strategy and research told BBC's Today programme: "This is where TikTok comes in. We found that some method of age checks being used by social media are not working well enough".At the heart of the regulator's probe into the platform is its use of technology known as "age inference".This essentially relies on estimating how old a user is based on how they use the platform, such as the videos they watch or others they interact with.Davies said Ofcom had "serious doubts" over whether such tools are good enough at checking the age of users.The regulator requires social media platforms, among others, to use "highly effective" methods to check users are old enough to use them and prevent children from seeing harmful material."We have very serious questions about whether age inference can be highly effective," she said.But a TikTok spokesperson said: "We strictly enforce age-appropriate experiences through expert-informed platform rules and advanced age inference technologies, in line with major industry peers."They said the company had invested "billions" in online safety since launching in the UK eight years ago.
contentParagraphs.zh: 已经对 TikTok 是否采取了足够措施阻止儿童进入其平台展开了调查。媒体监管机构 Ofcom 的调查是在英国政府宣布将完全禁止 16 岁以下儿童使用一系列平台之后一个月进行的。Ofcom 将检查该视频共享应用程序如何评估用户是否为儿童，以及它是否有足够的系统来防止儿童观看有害内容。TikTok 发言人表示：“我们有信心履行我们的《在线安全法》义务，并将与 Ofcom 合作证明这一点。”今年 5 月，监管机构批评该平台对儿童来说“不够安全”，并呼吁对儿童的在线安全采取更强有力的行动。Ofcom 战略与研究小组总监凯特·戴维斯 (Kate Davies) 告诉 BBC 的“今日”节目：“这就是 TikTok 的用武之地。我们发现社交媒体使用的某些年龄检查方法效果不够好。”监管机构对该平台进行调查的核心是其对“年龄推断”技术的使用。这本质上依赖于估计用户的年龄。戴维斯表示，Ofcom 对此类工具在检查用户年龄方面是否足够好表示“严重怀疑”。监管机构要求社交媒体平台等使用“高效”的方法来检查用户的年龄是否足以使用它们，并防止儿童看到有害材料。她说：“我们对年龄推断是否能够高效抱有非常严重的疑问。”但 TikTok 发言人表示：“我们严格执行通过专家知情的平台规则和先进的年龄推理技术，与主要行业同行保持一致，提供适合年龄的体验。”他们表示，自八年前在英国推出以来，该公司已在在线安全方面投资了“数十亿”。

articles.title: British Steel taken into public ownership to protect 'vital' UK supply
articles.summary: The Scunthorpe steelworks has been officially nationalised under new government powers passed this week.
articles.titleZh: 英国钢铁公司纳入公有制以保护英国“重要”供应
articles.summaryZh: 根据本周通过的新政府权力，斯肯索普钢厂已正式国有化。

contentParagraphs.en: British Steel has been taken into public ownership in what the government said was a bid to "protect UK steelmaking". The future of the steelworks, which employs roughly 2,700 people in Scunthorpe and supports many other industries in north Lincolnshire, has been dogged by uncertainty over recent years."Today's decision secures the future of steelmaking in the UK, protects skilled jobs and safeguards a vital national capability," Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer said. The UK government took control of British Steel operations in Scunthorpe last year, though it has since remained under the ownership of the Chinese firm Jingye Group.Jingye has said it has begun the process of seeking compensation for nationalisation, having previously said the business was losing £700,000 a day. The BBC has been unable to get a response from Jingye to Thursday's announcement.Starmer added: "British Steel is part of the fabric of our nation and a cornerstone of Britain's industrial strength."This government will always act in the national interest to support British industry, strengthen our economy and ensure the industries we rely on can thrive long into the future."In March, the National Audit Office released a report noting that the Scunthorpe steelworks was costing the government about £1.3m a day.The government had previously sought private investors to take control of the steel manufacturer, initially stopping short of full nationalisation."British Steel now belongs to the British people, and our focus is on the future: stabilising the business, backing the communities that rely on it and building a sustainable, competitive and decarbonised steel sector for the years ahead," Business Secretary Peter Kyle said.On Wednesday, Parliament passed legislation allowing the government to bring the steel industry into public ownership under circumstances where it met a public interest test.A spokesperson for the Department for Business and Trade had confirmed on Wednesday the government was "strongly minded" to use the new powers in the case of British Steel."The Steel Act gives us powers to nationalise steel companies where it's necessary in the public interest, to protect a foundation industry that supports our critical national infrastructure, economy and defence," the department said in a statement.Business Secretary Peter Kyle told the BBC the government will need to cover the running costs "for the immediate future".He said an independent assessor will determine whether Jingye should be compensated for the nationalisation based on the value of the company."But let me be really clear, there is an alternative here - that we let this business go bust," he said."If that business disappears, we will lose the ability for primary steel production in our country, we will become entirely dependent on global supply."The government seized control of the Scunthorpe plant in April 2025 after Jingye flagged the potential closure of the last two remaining blast furnaces.If the furnaces had been starved of fuel and gone out, the UK would no longer have had the capability to produce "virgin steel" because the process of restarting them would have been extremely difficult and costly.Simon Boyd, managing director of Reid Steel, a structural steel manufacturer in Dorset, said the nationalisation "had to be done".Boyd, whose company buys thousands of tonnes from British Steel each year, told the BBC's Today programme Jingye had been "sabotaging the infrastructure" at the company and the government "had to step in".He said the government would need to invest heavily in British Steel and would not see a return for 10-20 years.But he said it "now belongs to the British people". A bid to sell it on to private investors would have required government support, and in the past has been seen to "benefit the private companies and not the British people", he added.Virgin steel-making involves iron being extracted from its original source to be purified and treated to make all types of steel used in major construction projects, such as new buildings and railways.If the plant stopped producing virgin steel, the UK would become the only member of the G7 group of leading economies without the ability to make it. The government views that as a risk to the UK's economic security.Jingye bought British Steel in 2020 one year after it was placed in compulsory liquidation by its previous owner, private equity group Greybull Capital. Announcing the move to take British Steel into public ownership, the government said a strong domestic steel industry was "critical" to its plans to reindustrialise Britain and to "reduce reliance on overseas supply chains for strategically important materials".
contentParagraphs.zh: 英国钢铁公司已被纳入公有制，政府称这是为了“保护英国钢铁制造业”。该钢铁厂在斯肯索普雇佣了大约 2,700 名员工，并为北林肯郡的许多其他行业提供支持，近年来，该钢铁厂的未来一直受到不确定性的困扰。“今天的决定确保了英国钢铁制造业的未来，保护了熟练工作岗位并保障了至关重要的国家能力，”首相基尔·斯塔默爵士表示。英国政府去年控制了英国钢铁公司在斯肯索普的业务，但该业务此后仍由中国公司敬业集团所有。敬业表示，已开始寻求国有化赔偿，此前曾表示该业务每天损失 70 万英镑。 BBC一直未能得到敬业公司对周四声明的回应。斯塔默补充道：“英国钢铁公司是我们国家结构的一部分，也是英国工业实力的基石。本届政府将始终以国家利益为重，支持英国工业，增强我们的经济，确保我们所依赖的工业能够在未来长期蓬勃发展。”今年3月，国家审计署发布了一份报告，指出斯肯索普钢铁厂每天给政府造成约130万英镑的损失。政府此前曾寻求私人投资者控制这家钢铁制造商，最初没有完全国有化。“英国钢铁公司现在属于英国人民，我们的重点是未来：稳定业务，支持依赖它的社区，并在未来几年建立可持续、有竞争力和脱碳的钢铁行业。”商务大臣彼得·凯尔表示。周三，议会通过了一项立法，允许政府在满足公共利益测试的情况下将钢铁行业纳入公有制。周三证实，政府“坚决”在英国钢铁案中使用新权力。该部门在一份声明中表示，“《钢铁法》赋予我们在公共利益必要时将钢铁公司国有化的权力，以保护支持我们关键的国家基础设施、经济和国防的基础产业。”商务大臣彼得·凯尔告诉英国广播公司，政府将需要承担“近期”的运营成本。他表示，一名独立评估员将决定敬业是否应该因国有化而获得补偿他说：“但让我说清楚，这里有一个替代方案 - 我们让这家企业破产。”“如果该企业消失，我们将失去我国初级钢铁生产的能力，我们将完全依赖全球供应。”在敬业表示可能关闭最后两座高炉后，政府于 2025 年 4 月控制了斯肯索普工厂。如果这些高炉因缺乏燃料而熄火，英国将不会多塞特郡结构钢制造商里德钢铁公司的董事总经理西蒙·博伊德表示，国有化“必须完成”。博伊德的公司每年从英国钢铁公司购买数千吨钢材，他告诉英国广播公司的“今日”节目，敬业一直在该公司“破坏基础设施”，政府“必须介入”。他说，政府需要大量投资他补充说，英国钢铁公司“现在属于英国人民”，将其出售给私人投资者需要政府的支持，而在过去，这种做法被认为“有利于私人公司，而不是英国人民”。原始炼钢涉及从原始来源中提取铁，进行纯化和处理，以生产用于新建筑和铁路等重大建设项目的各种钢材。英国将成为七国集团主要经济体中唯一没有能力实现这一目标的成员。 政府认为，这对英国的经济安全构成风险。 在英国钢铁公司被其前所有者私募股权集团灰牛资本强制清算一年后，敬业集团收购了英国钢铁公司。政府表示，强大的国内钢铁业对其英国再工业化和“减少钢铁产量”的计划“至关重要”。具有重要战略意义的材料依赖海外供应链”。

articles.title: Fear and hope in Utah in the shadow of America's largest wildfire
articles.summary: People in Monticello, Utah have been tense and preparing to evacuate since shortly after the Babylon fire started June 26th
articles.titleZh: 美国最大野火阴影下的犹他州的恐惧与希望
articles.summaryZh: 自6月26日巴比伦大火爆发后不久，犹他州蒙蒂塞洛的人们就一直处于紧张状态并准备撤离

contentParagraphs.en: The Babylon fire burns on the Manti-La Sal National Forest outside Monticello, Utah July 6, 2026
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026年7月6日，犹他州蒙蒂塞洛郊外的Manti-La Sal国家森林被巴比伦大火烧毁

contentParagraphs.en: Since it started three weeks ago the Babylon Fire has consumed more than 106,000 acres of forest in the mountains outside the town of Monticello, Utah. People there remain ready to evacuate at a moment's notice.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自三周前开始以来，巴比伦大火已经在犹他州蒙蒂塞洛镇外的山区吞噬了超过106,000英亩的森林。那里的人们随时准备撤离。

contentParagraphs.en: For the last week, dozens of Monticello residents have been gathering every evening on the west edge of town, to thank and support some of the hundreds of firefighters here when a long line of their trucks roll out of the Abajo Mountains.
contentParagraphs.zh: 上周，数十名蒙蒂塞洛居民每天晚上都聚集在城镇的西部边缘，感谢和支持这里数百名消防员中的一些人，他们的卡车从阿巴霍山脉驶出。

contentParagraphs.en: People here have been through a lot since the Babylon Fire started June 26th, when dry conditions and 50 mile-per-hour winds blew the fire up quickly.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自6月26日巴比伦大火开始以来，这里的人们经历了很多，当时干燥的条件和每小时50英里的风迅速吹起了大火。

contentParagraphs.en: "The first few days it was moving 20,000 acres a day," said Monticello mayor Kevin Dunn. "Almost a thousand acres an hour. At that point it was completely uncontrollable."
contentParagraphs.zh: 蒙蒂塞洛市市长凯文·邓恩（ Kevin Dunn ）说： “最初几天，它每天移动2万英亩土地。” “几乎每小时一千英亩。那时候是完全无法控制的。”

contentParagraphs.en: Initially the fire grew inside a buffer of federal land on the Manti-LaSal National Forest. But by July 6 a roiling tower of dark smoke appeared just over the mountain a few miles west of the city.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最初，火势在Manti-LaSal国家森林的联邦土地缓冲区内蔓延。但到了7月6日，城市以西几英里的山上出现了一座滚滚的黑烟塔。

contentParagraphs.en: 'Truly apocalyptic'
contentParagraphs.zh: “真正的世界末日”

contentParagraphs.en: "It literally (looked) like there's been an atomic explosion back there," Dunn said, remembering that afternoon. "There's lightning flashing everywhere from the particles in the upper atmosphere. The town was completely engulfed in ash. We had pine needles falling, leaves partially burned falling all over town. It was truly apocalyptic."
contentParagraphs.zh: “它真的（看起来）像是那里发生了原子弹爆炸，”邓恩说，记得那天下午。“高层大气中的粒子到处闪烁着闪电。镇上完全被灰烬吞没。我们有松针落下，部分烧焦的叶子落在整个城镇。这真的是世界末日。”

contentParagraphs.en: As he watched from his yard, Dunn fielded calls from anxious residents.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当他从院子里看时，邓恩接到了焦虑的居民的电话。

contentParagraphs.en: "I had several people calling me, you know, 'What are we doing mayor? What's happening? How are we going to protect our lives and our town?'" said Dunn. "I said, 'well, get your stuff ready to go. If we need to leave, we'll leave.'"
contentParagraphs.zh: “我有几个人打电话给我，你知道， '我们在做什么，市长？发生了什么？我们将如何保护我们的生命和我们的城镇？'”邓恩说。“我说， '好吧，把你的东西准备好。如果我们需要离开，我们会离开。'”

contentParagraphs.en: Officials put much of the town of about 2,000 people on notice: Be ready to evacuate immediately.
contentParagraphs.zh: 官员们提醒全镇约2000人：做好立即疏散的准备。

contentParagraphs.en: Kevin Dunn is the mayor of Monticello, Utah
contentParagraphs.zh: 凯文·邓恩（ Kevin Dunn ）是犹他州蒙蒂塞洛市市长

contentParagraphs.en: Adam Burke/KSUT
contentParagraphs.zh: Adam Burke/KSUT

contentParagraphs.en: Adam Burke/KSUT
contentParagraphs.zh: Adam Burke/KSUT

contentParagraphs.en: "We've been ready to go for over a week," said Bonnie Des Rosiers, who moved to Monticello from Florida about 25 years ago. "Luckily it hasn't gotten to the go stage."
contentParagraphs.zh: 大约25年前从佛罗里达州搬到蒙蒂塞洛的Bonnie Des Rosiers说： “我们已经准备好了一个多星期。” “幸运的是，它还没有到达起飞阶段。”

contentParagraphs.en: Des Rosiers has watched some of her neighbors filling their cars with as many belongings as they could pack in.
contentParagraphs.zh: Des Rosiers看到她的一些邻居在他们的车里装满了他们可以收拾的东西。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's your whole life," she said. "You hate to think of it going up in flames."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这是你的一生，”她说。“你讨厌想到它在火焰中燃烧。”

contentParagraphs.en: To date, federal agencies have spent nearly $42 million dollars on the Babylon Fire, bringing in helicopters, air tankers, drones, and more than 1500 firefighters. Crews carved a firebreak in the oak brush west of Monticello.
contentParagraphs.zh: 迄今为止，联邦机构在巴比伦大火上花费了近4200万美元，引进了直升机、空中加油机、无人机和1500多名消防员。船员在蒙蒂塞洛以西的橡树丛中雕刻了一道防火墙。

contentParagraphs.en: Some relief, but still dangerous
contentParagraphs.zh: 有些松了一口气，但仍然很危险

contentParagraphs.en: Then the weather changed, thunderstorms rolled in. The fire is now 54% contained.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然后天气变了，雷雨滚滚而来。火势现已遏制54%。

contentParagraphs.en: "Mother nature sprinkling a little water out there? I'll take it," said Mack MacFarland at a July 13 community meeting in Monticello.
contentParagraphs.zh: “大自然母亲往外面洒点水？我接受，”麦克·麦克法兰在7月13日在蒙蒂塞洛举行的社区会议上说。

contentParagraphs.en: With 34 years of firefighting experience, MacFarland recently took over leadership of the federal response to the Babylon Fire. He reminded the public at his first briefing for them, that just a few weeks ago, 3 firefighters died in the Knowles Fire in western Colorado.
contentParagraphs.zh: MacFarland拥有34年的消防经验，最近接管了联邦应对巴比伦大火的领导权。他在为他们举行的第一次简报会上提醒公众，就在几周前， 3名消防员在科罗拉多州西部的诺尔斯大火中丧生。

contentParagraphs.en: "Interview after interview with people involved in that fire, said, 'I have never seen in all my years in fire the fuels and fire behavior that we saw there.'" MacFarland said. "So there's something different this year. I am not going to let down my guard because we have a little bit of rain out there."
contentParagraphs.zh: “在接受参与火灾的人的采访后，他说： '我多年来从未见过我们在那里看到的燃料和火灾行为。'”麦克法兰说， “所以今年会有所不同。我不会放松警惕，因为外面下了一点雨。”

contentParagraphs.en: But with moisture in the air, and less ominous smoke, Monticello residents are feeling optimistic.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但由于空气中的水分和不祥的烟雾，蒙蒂塞洛居民感到乐观。

contentParagraphs.en: People in Monticello, Utah show support for firefighters returning from their shifts battling the Babylon fire in the mountains west of town
contentParagraphs.zh: 犹他州蒙蒂塞洛的人们表示支持消防员下班返回，与城西山区的巴比伦大火作斗争

contentParagraphs.en: Adam Burke/KSUT
contentParagraphs.zh: Adam Burke/KSUT

contentParagraphs.en: Adam Burke/KSUT
contentParagraphs.zh: Adam Burke/KSUT

contentParagraphs.en: Last night, on the edge of town about 45 people cheered and waved homemade signs as firefighters came in from the mountains.
contentParagraphs.zh: 昨晚，当消防员从山上进来时，大约有45人在城边欢呼并挥舞着自制标志。

contentParagraphs.en: Some firefighters smile, honk their horns; others can barely muster an exhausted nod.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些消防员微笑着按喇叭；其他人几乎无法点头示意。

contentParagraphs.en: This has helped," said Adriann Goodwine, who lives in the neighborhood. "Coming out every night and saying thank you, and saying thank you, and seeing their faces and waving to them and showing them our love. That helped us get through."
contentParagraphs.zh: 住在附近的Adriann Goodwine说，这很有帮助。“每天晚上出来说谢谢，说谢谢，看到他们的脸，向他们挥手，向他们表达我们的爱。这有助于我们度过难关。”

contentParagraphs.en: As of Wednesday evening, the town of Monticello has lowered the evacuation warning, from "set" to "ready" status, which means residents still must be prepared to evacuate if necessary.
contentParagraphs.zh: 截至周三晚上，蒙蒂塞洛镇已将疏散警告从“设置”降至“准备就绪”状态，这意味着居民仍必须准备好在必要时撤离。

contentParagraphs.en: But the fire is still dangerous. How much longer it will threaten Monticello will depend on the weather.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但火灾仍然很危险。它将对蒙蒂塞洛造成多长时间的威胁将取决于天气。

articles.title: Yemen is edging closer to renewed confrontation
articles.summary: The Iranian aircraft incident has exposed a fragile calm that may not survive a wider regional escalation.
articles.titleZh: 也门正接近重新爆发对抗
articles.summaryZh: 伊朗飞机事件暴露了脆弱的平静，这种平静可能无法在更广泛的地区升级中幸存下来。

contentParagraphs.en: In the space of just a few days, Yemen appeared to be slipping out of its fragile de-escalation and back into the heart of regional tensions.The incident involving the Iranian aircraft that entered Yemeni airspace was not merely a dispute over a flight. It exposed the extent to which the war in Yemen has become intertwined with the confrontation between the United States and Iran, and raised an old question in a new form: Can Yemen remain outside any broader regional escalation?The Iranian aircraft’s attempt to land in Sanaa, the subsequent targeting of the airport runway to prevent it from doing so and its eventual landing in Hodeidah opened a dispute that extends far beyond civil aviation.The internationally recognised Yemeni government and Saudi Arabia treated the flight as a test of sovereignty and of Iran’s ability to establish a direct link with Houthi-controlled areas. The Houthis and Tehran, meanwhile, presented it as an attempt to break the restrictions imposed on Sanaa.The Houthis’ response against Saudi Arabia was therefore not entirely unexpected. Their targeting of Abha airport in retaliation for the strike on Sanaa airport that prevented the aircraft from landing marked the first Houthi-claimed attack on Saudi Arabia since the informal truce began in March 2022.So far, however, the attack has remained limited, and Saudi Arabia has not responded with a full-scale military campaign. This suggests that, despite the heightened escalation, both sides are still proceeding with careful calculations.It was in this context that the US Department of State responded to a question from a television channel about reports that President Donald Trump had given Saudi Arabia the green light to act against the Houthis.Washington did not explicitly confirm those reports. It said it was monitoring them, before firmly affirming its support for Saudi Arabia in confronting what it described as Iranian aggression, including attacks by the Iran-backed Houthis.More importantly, the State Department directly linked Houthi attacks and the threats the group has issued to core US interests in the region, foremost among them freedom of navigation in the Red Sea and preventing the export of terrorism.This language does not mean that a decision to go to war has been made. But it does indicate that the Houthis’ place in US calculations has changed.The group is no longer viewed solely as a Yemeni actor, but as part of Iran’s network of influence, capable of threatening both Saudi Arabia and the Red Sea.Against this backdrop, Saudi Chief of the General Staff General Fayyad al-Ruwaili met Lieutenant-General Patrick Frank, deputy commander of US Central Command.The official announcement referred to developing military cooperation and did not mention Yemen. But the timing of the meeting gives it additional significance. It came after the attack on Saudi Arabia, the Iranian aircraft crisis and amid an ongoing escalation between the US and Iran.The situation, then, cannot be separated from the region’s wider tensions. Iran is under direct pressure in its confrontation with the United States, and the Strait of Hormuz lies at the heart of those tensions, while the Bab al-Mandab, the narrow strait linking the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden and a vital trade route between Europe and Asia, remains one of the region’s most sensitive sources of leverage.Should the confrontation expand, the Houthis would be among Iran’s allies best placed to open another pressure front.This is what gives the military movements in Marib, al-Jawf, Harf Sufyan and elsewhere in Yemen their importance.There is not yet sufficient evidence that a decision has been made to launch a major ground war. But forces are being placed on heightened alert and mobilised across several fronts at an extremely sensitive moment. As regional escalation intensifies, any internal confrontation becomes more likely to expand.In the opposite direction, United Nations Special Envoy Hans Grundberg travelled to Muscat, where he met Omani officials and Mohammed Abdul Salam, the head of the Houthi negotiating delegation.His primary mission now appears to be not to launch a comprehensive settlement, but to prevent this round of escalation from turning into a new war.De-escalation, the attack on Saudi Arabia, Iranian flights and the rules governing the use of Sanaa airport are likely to be at the heart of the current discussions, even if their details have not been made public. Those efforts may succeed, or they may not.Calm remains the most likely scenario in the short term. Saudi Arabia does not appear eager to return quickly to a broad war, while the Houthis understand that an open confrontation with the kingdom, coinciding with the US-Israel war on Iran, could expose them to an entirely different level of pressure.The responses may therefore remain measured. The escalation may stop at a certain point, and Muscat may succeed in bringing both parties back towards restraint. But that does not mean the underlying causes of the confrontation have disappeared.The Iranian aircraft incident exposed the fragility of the current de-escalation. The targeting of Abha airport reopened the Saudi front. The US position has also become clearer in linking the Houthis to Iran and to security in the Red Sea.Meanwhile, military movements on the ground indicate that the parties are not behaving as though a settlement is close.For these reasons, I believe the confrontation has been postponed, not cancelled. Any expansion of the regional conflict will make it increasingly difficult to keep Yemen out of it.The views expressed in this article are the author’s own and do not necessarily reflect Al Jazeera’s editorial policy.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在短短几天的时间里，也门似乎正在摆脱脆弱的局势缓和，重新陷入地区紧张局势的中心。伊朗飞机进入也门领空的事件不仅仅是一次航班纠纷。它暴露了也门战争与美国和伊朗之间的对抗交织在一起的程度，并以新的形式提出了一个老问题：也门能否置身于更广泛的地区升级之外？伊朗飞机试图降落在萨那，随后瞄准机场跑道以阻止其降落，最终降落在荷台达，引发了一场远远超出民用航空范围的争端。国际公认的也门政府和沙特阿拉伯将这次飞行视为对主权和主权的考验。伊朗有能力与胡塞武装控制地区建立直接联系。与此同时，胡塞武装和德黑兰将其视为试图打破对萨那的限制。因此，胡塞武装对沙特阿拉伯的反应并不完全出乎意料。他们以艾卜哈机场为目标，以报复对萨那机场的袭击，导致飞机无法降落，这是自 2022 年 3 月非正式休战开始以来胡塞武装声称对沙特阿拉伯的首次袭击。然而，到目前为止，袭击仍然有限，沙特阿拉伯尚未采取全面的军事行动予以回应。这表明，尽管事态升级，双方仍在进行仔细的盘算。正是在这种背景下，美国国务院回应了电视频道有关特朗普总统已为沙特对胡塞武装采取行动的报道的提问。华盛顿方面没有明确证实这些报道。它表示正在监视这些行动，然后坚定地申明支持沙特阿拉伯对抗其所谓的伊朗侵略，包括伊朗支持的胡塞武装的袭击。更重要的是，国务院将胡塞武装的袭击与该组织对美国在该地区的核心利益发出的威胁直接联系起来，其中最重要的是红海航行自由和防止恐怖主义输出。这种措辞并不意味着已经做出了发动战争的决定。但这确实表明，胡塞武装在美国算盘中的地位已经发生了变化。该组织不再被仅仅视为也门势力，而是被视为伊朗影响力网络的一部分，能够威胁沙特和红海。在此背景下，沙特总参谋长法耶兹·鲁瓦利将军会见了美国中央司令部副司令帕特里克·弗兰克中将。官方声明中提到了发展军事合作，但没有提及也门。但会议的时机赋予了它额外的意义。此前，沙特阿拉伯遇袭、伊朗飞机危机，以及美国和伊朗之间的紧张局势持续升级。因此，当前局势与该地区更广泛的紧张局势密不可分。伊朗在与美国的对抗中面临直接压力，而霍尔木兹海峡是这些紧张局势的核心，而连接红海和亚丁湾的狭窄海峡以及欧洲和亚洲之间的重要贸易路线曼德海峡仍然是该地区最敏感的影响力来源之一。如果对抗扩大，胡塞武装将成为伊朗最有能力开辟另一条压力战线的盟友之一。这就是伊朗的军事行动的原因。马里卜、焦夫、哈夫苏菲安和也门其他地方的重要性。目前还没有足够的证据表明已经决定发动一场大规模地面战争。但在这个极其敏感的时刻，军队正处于高度戒备状态，并在多条战线上进行动员。随着地区局势升级，任何内部对抗都更有可能扩大。相反，联合国特使汉斯·格伦德伯格前往马斯喀特，会见了阿曼官员和胡塞谈判代表团团长穆罕默德·阿卜杜勒·萨拉姆。他现在的首要任务似乎不是启动全面解决方案，而是防止本轮升级演变成新的战争。降级、袭击沙特、伊朗航班和规则管理萨那机场的使用可能是当前讨论的核心，即使其细节尚未公开。这些努力可能会成功，也可能不会。短期内最有可能出现的情况是平静。沙特阿拉伯似乎并不急于迅速重返全面战争，而胡塞武装明白，与沙特的公开对抗，恰逢美以对伊朗的战争，可能会让他们面临完全不同程度的压力。因此，他们的反应可能会保持谨慎。升级可能会在某个时刻停止，而马斯喀特可能会成功地让双方恢复克制。但这并不意味着对抗的根本原因已经消失。伊朗飞机事件暴露了当前局势缓和的脆弱性。艾卜哈机场的袭击重新打开了沙特的战线。美国在将胡塞武装与伊朗和红海安全联系起来方面的立场也变得更加明确。与此同时，当地的军事行动表明，双方的表现似乎并没有接近解决。出于这些原因，我认为对抗已经被推迟，而不是取消。地区冲突的任何扩大都会使也门越来越难以被卷入其中。 本文表达的观点仅代表作者个人观点，并不一定反映半岛电视台的编辑政策。

articles.title: Robots, AI and drones: how the Dutch navy is using tech to transform its sea defences
articles.summary:
Uncrewed systems are the future for armed forces and the Netherlands is leading the way ‘to keep people out of danger zones’
On each side of the target ship, a black vessel keeps a watchful distance. Defender 1 and Defender 2 are the eyes and ears of the navy – but they have nobo
articles.titleZh: 机器人、人工智能和无人机：荷兰海军如何利用技术改造其海上防御
articles.summaryZh:
无人系统是武装部队的未来，荷兰正在引领“让人们远离危险区域”的道路
目标船两侧各有一艘黑色船只保持警惕距离。 Defender 1 和 Defender 2 是海军的眼睛和耳朵 – 但他们没有

contentParagraphs.en: On each side of the target ship, a black vessel keeps a watchful distance. Defender 1 and Defender 2 are the eyes and ears of the navy – but they have nobody onboard, and their paths are controlled by a computer system.This is the future of the Royal Netherlands Navy, according to Capt Sjoerd Feenstra, head of the expertise centre for unmanned systems. He is leading a five-week mission, off the coast of Den Helder in the north of the country, to test the limits of systems that operate without the human touch.“For the last year and a half we’ve been working to change our organisation,” he says. “In about 10 years there will be crewed platforms surrounded by a ring of uncrewed systems operating as autonomously as possible.”Underwater vehicles, boats and drones – all uncrewed – are expected to play a key role in future armed forces around the world. The latest Dutch military budget promises to use uncrewed systems for more than half of its work within five years. The UK, in a similar period, plans to spend more than £5bn on such technology.Defender 2 during uncrewed trials off the coast of Den Helder in June. Photograph: John van Helvert/Media Centrum DefensieCentral to all this is the GeoSea vessel. Once used to monitor the seabed around windfarms, it is now the base for testing Noa drones – which look like giant flies – the Defender vessels and a Lobster Robotics undersea mine mapper. The “system of systems” is designed so that new models can be swapped in and out as technology advances.Drones are already used in live conflicts. Their use and capability have increased exponentially since Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022. They have played a key role in Iranian strikes, and in the war in the Middle East, while the US Navy deployed unmanned sea vessels this year in operations against Iran.For the Dutch navy, uncrewed systems are more than a trend. “The goal is to do as many things as possible with unmanned systems to keep people out of danger zones,” says Feenstra. “The work has got a lot more difficult, especially with the amount of information, speed and capacity demanded. And some jobs are unbelievably boring.”On the command to “monitor that ship”, for instance, the Defender vessels are deployed, two carbon-fibre Noa drones are dispatched and another drone resembling a huge bat flies high overhead.The GeoSea testing base and drone during the Den Helder trials last month. Photograph: John van Helvert/Media Centrum DefensieTechnical failure is always a risk. Artificial intelligence can hallucinate, or generate a false result, says the software integration lead Ferdinand Peters. “You need to let the system work for you, but not think for you,” he says. “We need to think carefully about where we use it and where we do not.”A machine will not decide to pull the trigger here. “A person is always part of the decision-making chain,” says Feenstra.The ethical question isn’t new, either: for more than 40 years, the Netherlands has had the Goalkeeper air defence system, which can function autonomously.“What we know from the past is that even when people make the plan or collect the information, something can go wrong,” says Feenstra. “The question is where the culpability will lie if you automate everything.”It makes sense for a country with a labour shortage to aim for more autonomy, but the Dutch are an inspiration to other countries collaborating on security in the North and Baltic seas, according to Lee Willett, a naval analyst.One of the Dutch navy’s bat-shaped drones. Photograph: John van Helvert/Media Centrum Defensie“The Dutch punch above their weight,” Willett says. “They are also advancing what they have because they recognise that they are a relatively small navy in an incredibly important part of the world.”Sidharth Kaushal, senior research fellow for sea power at the Royal United Services Institute thinktank in London says he believes the tide has turned towards such systems – not least, because they reduce long stretches at sea.“Uncrewed systems don’t completely remove the requirement for manpower – you tend to need more engineers – but they provide a different balance in terms of family life,” he says. “This is the direction of travel.”
contentParagraphs.zh: 目标船两侧各有一艘黑色船只保持警惕距离。 “卫士 1”和“卫士 2”是海军的眼睛和耳朵，但船上无人，其路径由计算机系统控制。无人系统专业中心负责人 Sjoerd Feenstra 上尉表示，这就是荷兰皇家海军的未来。他正在该国北部的登海尔德海岸领导一项为期五周的任务，以测试在没有人为接触的情况下运行的系统的极限。“在过去的一年半里，我们一直在努力改变我们的组织，”他说。 “大约十年后，载人平台将被一圈尽可能自主运行的无人系统包围。” 水下车辆、船只和无人机——全部无人——预计将在世界各地未来的武装部队中发挥关键作用。荷兰最新的军事预算承诺在五年内使用无人系统完成一半以上的工作。同期，英国计划在此类技术上花费超过 50 亿英镑。卫士 2 号于 6 月在登海尔德海岸进行了无人试验。照片：John van Helvert/Media Centrum Defensie 这一切的中心是 GeoSea 船。它曾经用于监测风电场周围的海底，现在是测试 Noa 无人机（看起来像巨型苍蝇）、Defender 船只和 Lobster Robotics 海底雷测绘仪的基地。 “系统的系统”的设计使得随着技术的进步可以更换新的模型。无人机已经在现场冲突中使用。自2022年俄罗斯入侵乌克兰以来，它们的使用和能力呈指数级增长。它们在伊朗袭击和中东战争中发挥了关键作用，而美国海军今年在针对伊朗的行动中部署了无人海上舰艇。对于荷兰海军来说，无人系统不仅仅是一种趋势。 “我们的目标是利用无人系统做尽可能多的事情，让人们远离危险区域，”费恩斯特拉说。 “这项工作变得更加困难，特别是对信息量、速度和容量的要求。有些工作令人难以置信地无聊。”例如，在执行“监视那艘船”的命令时，部署了“卫士”船只，出动了两架碳纤维诺亚无人机，另一架类似巨大蝙蝠的无人机高高飞过。上个月登海尔德试验期间的GeoSea测试基地和无人机。照片：John van Helvert/Media Centrum Defensie 技术故障总是存在风险。软件集成主管费迪南德·彼得斯 (Ferdinand Peters) 表示，人工智能可能会产生幻觉，或产生错误的结果。 “你需要让系统为你工作，而不是为你思考，”他说。 “我们需要仔细考虑在哪里使用它，在哪里不使用它。”机器不会决定在这里扣动扳机。 Feenstra 说：“人始终是决策链的一部分。”道德问题也不是什么新鲜事：40 多年来，荷兰一直拥有可以自主运行的“守门员”防空系统。“我们从过去知道，即使人们制定计划或收集信息，也可能会出现问题，”Feenstra 说。 “问题在于，如果一切都实现自动化，罪魁祸首将在哪里。” 海军分析家李·威利特表示，对于一个劳动力短缺的国家来说，寻求更多自主权是有道理的，但荷兰人对其他国家在北海和波罗的海的安全合作具有启发意义。荷兰海军的一种蝙蝠形无人机。照片：John van Helvert/Media Centrum Defensie “荷兰队的拳头超出了他们的实力，”威利特说道。 “他们也在推进现有的能力，因为他们认识到，在世界上一个极其重要的地区，他们是一支规模相对较小的海军。”伦敦皇家联合三军研究所智囊团海权高级研究员西德哈斯·考沙尔(Sidharth Kaushal)表示，他相信潮流已经转向这种系统，尤其是因为它们减少了长时间的海上航行。“无人系统并不能完全消除对人力的需求——你往往需要更多的工程师——但它们在家庭生活方面提供了不同的平衡，”他说。 “这就是行进的方向。”

articles.title: Celebrity influencers paid up to £1m to advertise deodorant on Instagram
articles.summary: The chief executive of Wild reveals what happens behind the scenes of social media posts.
articles.titleZh: 名人影响者支付高达 100 万英镑在 Instagram 上为除臭剂做广告
articles.summaryZh: Wild 首席执行官揭示了社交媒体帖子幕后发生的事情。

contentParagraphs.en: How much the company spends on influencer marketing "can vary from £100,000 through to millions if you want a top-tier celebrity", says Bowes-Lyon.He says a lot of it also depends on the depth of the campaign as "if you want them to do a one-off post you wouldn't pay too much but typically what they and you want is to develop is a bit more of a relationship".British tennis player Emma Raducanu is Wild's current brand ambassador and has been working with the brand for the past year. "We've done tons with her like full day shoots in New York, lots of posts and stories on Instagram and she even came in to create her own deodorant scent," says Bowes-Lyon."These celebrities aren't strapped for cash so it's not really about the money for them, it's more about whether the brand is a right fit," he says. "When I tell people influencers make £2,000 for a single post, they are shocked, never mind the fact some are making £50,000 for one post."Katy Howell, director at marketing agency Rethink Social, says paid ads "don't necessarily corrupt a recommendation but it changes the context in which that recommendation should be understood, and audiences have a right to know that"."The real test is whether the creator would plausibly use the product, whether they retain enough editorial freedom to express reservations, and whether the partnership fits the relationship they have built with their audience," she adds.Bowes-Lyon accepts audiences have become more aware that influencer ads are paid for which makes authenticity even more important. "People are getting a little bit more savvy and if for example a gaming influencer starts talking about a flower shop or something that is not linked to anything they do, people will see through that," he says.He adds that someone like TV personality Stacey Solomon has built her influence by being selective about what she promotes."I would imagine Stacey probably rejects 98% of the pitches that come her way."Wild's campaign with Solomon created more than 100 paid ad iterations for social media each with unique selling positionings to appeal to different audiences.Recent ASA research, external found that influencer advertising remained considerably harder to identify than conventional brand advertising. In its testing, 74% of participants were certain that a conventional brand post was an advert, compared with only 54% for influencer advertising.Bowes-Lyon says the influencers they work with need to "believe in what they're saying, not just saying it because they're getting a cheque". He points to a partnership with Molly-Mae Hague, who had used Wild's body wash in a video before the brand approached her."We'd never even spoken to her and she was using our body wash in it, so we then reached out."Howell suspects that "many people understand that influencers are paid, but underestimate both the scale and complexity of the economics"."The most prominent creators are no longer simply individuals posting from their bedrooms. Many operate as sophisticated media businesses, with managers, agents, production teams, legal advice, licensing arrangements and several income streams."While adverts on social media must be clearly labelled, Howell says that #ad is "legally functional but informationally thin" so more needs to be done to make paid marketing clearer.
contentParagraphs.zh: Bowes-Lyon 表示，该公司在影响者营销上的花费“如果你想要顶级名人，可以从 10 万英镑到数百万英镑不等”。他说，这在很大程度上还取决于营销活动的深度，因为“如果你想让他们一次性发帖，你不会花太多钱，但通常他们和你想要的是发展一种更多的关系”。 英国网球运动员艾玛·拉杜卡努 (Emma Raducanu) 是 Wild 现任品牌大使，并一直在与过去一年的品牌。 “我们和她一起做了很多事情，比如在纽约进行一整天的拍摄，在 Instagram 上发了很多帖子和故事，她甚至还来创造自己的除臭剂香水，”鲍斯-里昂说。“这些名人并不缺钱，所以对他们来说，钱并不重要，更重要的是这个品牌是否合适，”他说。 “当我告诉人们有影响力的人发一篇帖子就能赚 2,000 英镑时，他们会感到震惊，更不用说有些人一篇帖子就能赚 50,000 英镑。”营销机构 Rethink Social 总监凯蒂·豪厄尔 (Katy Howell) 表示，付费广告“不一定会破坏推荐，但它会改变应该理解该推荐的背景，观众有权知道这一点”。“真正的考验是创作者是否会合理地使用该产品，他们是否保留足够的编辑自由来表达预订，以及合作伙伴关系是否符合他们与受众建立的关系，”她补充道。Bowes-Lyon 承认受众已经越来越意识到影响者广告是付费的，这使得真实性变得更加重要。他说：“人们变得更加精明了，如果一位游戏影响者开始谈论一家花店或与他们所做的任何事情无关的事情，人们就会看穿这一点。”他补充说，电视名人史黛西·所罗门（Stacey Solomon）这样的人通过对她所宣传的内容进行有选择性的选择来建立自己的影响力。“我想史黛西可能会拒绝她收到的 98% 的推销。”Wild 与所罗门的竞选活动创造了 100 多个付费广告迭代每个社交媒体都有独特的销售定位来吸引不同的受众。外部 ASA 最近的研究发现，影响者广告仍然比传统品牌广告更难识别。在测试中，74% 的参与者确信传统品牌帖子是广告，而对于有影响力的广告，只有 54% 的参与者确信这一点。 Bowes-Lyon 表示，与他们合作的有影响力的人需要“相信他们所说的话，而不仅仅是因为他们得到一张支票而说出来”。他提到了与 Molly-Mae Hague 的合作，在该品牌联系 Wild 之前，她曾在一段视频中使用过 Wild 的沐浴露。“我们甚至从未和她说过话，她在里面使用了我们的沐浴露，所以我们就联系了她。”Howell 怀疑，“很多人都知道网红是有报酬的，但低估了经济的规模和复杂性”。“最杰出的创作者不再只是在卧室里发帖的个人。许多创作者都是复杂的媒体企业，拥有经理、代理人、制作团队、法律咨询、许可安排和多种收入来源。”虽然社交媒体上的广告必须有明确的标签，但豪厄尔表示，#ad“具有合法功能，但信息贫乏”，因此需要做更多工作来使付费营销更加清晰。

articles.title: Canada wildfires leave train 'encased in flames' as smoke drifts towards US
articles.summary: The crew was rescued after the close call. Meanwhile, blazes in Canada have left Toronto under a haze and smoke heading towards several major US cities.
articles.titleZh: 加拿大野火使火车“被火焰包围”，烟雾飘向美国
articles.summaryZh: 船员们在千钧一发后获救。与此同时，加拿大的大火使多伦多笼罩在阴霾和烟雾中，向美国几个主要城市蔓延。

contentParagraphs.en: Dramatic video shows a freight train in Canada surrounded by wildfire flames with workers requesting an emergency rescue as they report being "encased" in fire."This could potentially overtake us here, this has gotten a little scary," a crew member says as the train rushes past burning trees near Armstrong, Ontario. There are more than 100 wildfires burning in the province, with several northwestern communities under threat. Smoke blanketed Toronto, which woke up to hazy skies and some of the worst air quality in the world. The wildfires have led to concerns smoke will drift into US cities over the next few days - including in New Jersey, where Sunday's World Cup final is due to take place.Canadian National Rail said in a statement that the workers who were trapped outside Armstrong were all safely rescued, and that operations have been temporarily suspended in the region.The statement did not add any further details about how the rescue occurred. There are currently 838 wildfires actively burning across Canada, according to the Canadian Interagency Forest Fire Centre.Grand Council Chief Linda Debassige said in a statement on Wednesday that the remote Namaygoosisagagun First Nation in northern Ontario was "devastated by an unexpected and fast-moving wildfire" that severely damaged homes and community buildings. More than a dozen fires are also burning in Minnesota, a US state on the Canadian border, due to extreme heat, dry conditions, and strong winds.Officials have warned people to stay indoors to avoid the smoke, and to avoid strenuous activity and heavy breathing when spending time outside.
contentParagraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

contentParagraphs.en: On Wednesday, residents of Canada's largest city, which has also faced extreme heat this week, woke up to a yellow sky, and warnings from Toronto officials about the dangerous air quality.IQAir, a company which tracks global air quality, ranked it as having the worst air quality in the world. It was ranked ahead of Kinshasa in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Delhi, Dubai, and Jerusalem.Outdoor World Cup fan events for the England and Argentina match on Wednesday were cancelled in the city, and wading pools closed. In the US, officials have warned that the smoke is expected to drift into the region starting on Wednesday, even as the extreme heat starts to ease.Extreme heat warnings in the midwestern states of Wisconsin and Minnesota are expected to expire by Thursday night.But air quality will remain an issue for millions, especially for children, the elderly, or those with health conditions."You may experience mild and common symptoms such as eye, nose and throat irritation, headaches or a mild cough," Environment Canada warned. "More serious but less common symptoms include wheezing, chest pains or severe cough. If you think you are having a medical emergency, seek immediate medical assistance." Officials say the smoke will not be as bad as it was in 2023, when smoke from Quebec wildfires blanketed the US East Coast. "At this time, current forecasts do not indicate a repeat of 2023 conditions," wrote New York City Emergency Management in a statement on Tuesday.The statement added that KN95 masks will be distributed for free at public library branches around the city.
