articles.title: US military to start screening for testosterone deficiency, Hegseth says
articles.summary: Hegseth says he is authorising hormone screening to ensure US troops "operate at your absolute best".
articles.titleZh: 赫格斯说，美国军方将开始筛查睾酮缺乏症
articles.summaryZh: 赫格斯表示，他正在授权进行激素筛查，以确保美军“以绝对最佳状态作战”。

contentParagraphs.en: US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth has announced that military personnel aged 30 and older will undergo testing for testosterone deficiency as part of annual health screenings.In a video posted on X, captioned "High-T Department", Hegseth said he was authorising the screening programme for troops to ensure "you have the right testosterone levels to operate at your absolute best".Troops with low testosterone levels will be offered voluntary hormone replacement therapy. Chief Pentagon Spokesman Sean Parnell said: "All Active Duty and Reserve Component personnel aged 30 and older will undergo mandatory screening for testosterone deficiency during their Periodic Health Assessment."Personnel younger than age 30 would also be able to request the screening voluntarily, Parnell added. "We owe our warriors the absolute best medical care in the world, and this programme delivers on that obligation," Hegseth explained in Wednesday's video. "Taking care of your long-term health means ensuring you remain strong, resilient and capable - not just for your next deployment, but for the rest of your life, so you can thrive long after you take off the uniform," he added.Taking testosterone for non-medical reasons, like artificial muscle enhancement without a doctor's prescription, is strictly prohibited in the military.Hegseth said in the video that the new programme was "not about artificial enhancement".In his written statement, Parnell added: "The protocol will enable the Department to establish a comprehensive baseline and offer targeted testosterone therapy, ensuring that it sustains a healthy, capable, and decisively dominant fighting force."Asked whether both male and female troops would be tested, and if female troops would be able to be evaluated for oestrogen-based therapy as they enter perimenopause, the Pentagon said it had no comment to add to Hegseth and Parnell's statements.Democratic Senator Tammy Duckworth of Illinois, an Iraq war veteran and member of the armed services committee, called on the defence secretary to make hormone testing available for both men and women.Congresswoman Chrissy Houlahan, a Pennsylvania Democrat and Air Force veteran, dismissed it on X as Hegseth's "latest culture-war obsession".Levels of testosterone increase during puberty. The hormone helps boys to develop muscle strength and body hair as well as make sperm.At some point during the window between ages 30 and 40, men's testosterone levels naturally start to decline.Estimates vary, but low testosterone is thought to affect 10-40% of older men globally, external - which is thought to translate to about 13 million men in the US.Those men may experience a low mood, reduced sex drive and loss of muscle mass.A blood test can check the testosterone level and treatment can be given.Dr Mohit Khera, who led a US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) expert panel last year on screening and testosterone utilisation in the military, told the BBC that all men over 30 should get screened because it is the top marker of a person's overall current and future health."The key here is that many young men have low testosterone levels, which puts them at a disadvantage in terms of muscle mass, energy, and that could be an issue if you are in combat," Khera, a professor of urology at Baylor College of Medicine, said.But he added: "You have to be careful not to just give someone testosterone unless they do have some kind of symptoms."The benefits of the hormone replacement therapy, he explained, are increased muscle mass, decreased fat deposition, and decreased risk for depression. Over time, Khera added, it can also help with bone mineral density.He cautioned, though, that it is not meant for everyone."If young men do take testosterone, it can make them infertile," Khera said. "You got to be careful. If someone's in the reproductive years, you need to make sure they understand it's going to make you infertile."It can also result in a theoretical increased cardiovascular risk, he noted.Hegseth's announcement comes after US Health Secretary Robert F Kennedy Jr and other administration officials have sought to remove barriers for medical professionals to prescribe men testosterone. The FDA last month requested the removal of safety and effectiveness language on testosterone replacement therapy product labelling, and proposed loosening prescribing limits for testosterone products.Additional reporting by Michelle Roberts
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国国防部长皮特·赫格斯 (Pete Hegseth) 宣布，30 岁及以上的军人将接受睾酮缺乏症检测，作为年度健康筛查的一部分。在 X 上发布的一段标题为“High-T Department”的视频中，赫格斯 (Hegseth) 表示，他正在授权对部队进行筛查计划，以确保“你拥有正确的睾酮水平，以达到最佳状态”。睾酮水平较低的部队将自愿接受激素替代治疗。五角大楼首席发言人肖恩·帕内尔 (Sean Parnell) 表示：“所有 30 岁及以上的现役和预备役人员都将在定期健康评估期间接受睾丸激素缺乏症的强制筛查。”帕内尔补充说，30 岁以下的人员也可以自愿要求进行筛查。赫格斯在周三的视频中解释说：“我们应该为我们的战士提供世界上绝对最好的医疗服务，而这个计划履行了这一义务。”他补充说：“照顾你的长期健康意味着确保你保持强壮、有弹性和有能力——不仅仅是为了你的下一次部署，而是为了你的余生，这样你在脱下制服后也能长期茁壮成长。”在军队中严禁出于非医疗原因服用睾酮，比如未经医生处方的人工肌肉增强。赫格斯在视频中表示，新计划“不是人工增强”。在他的书面声明中，帕内尔补充道：“该协议将使国防部能够建立全面的基线并提供有针对性的睾丸激素治疗，确保其维持一支健康、有能力和具有决定性优势的战斗部队。”当被问及男性和女性士兵是否都会接受测试，以及女性士兵在进入围绝经期时是否能够接受基于雌激素的治疗评估时，五角大楼表示，它对赫格斯和帕内尔的声明没有任何评论。伊利诺伊州民主党参议员、伊拉克战争退伍军人、武装部队委员会成员塔米·达克沃斯呼吁国防部长要求对男性和女性进行激素检测。宾夕法尼亚州民主党人、空军退伍军人、国会女议员克丽丝·霍拉汉 (Chrissy Houulahan) 在 X 上将其斥为赫格斯“最新的文化战争痴迷”。青春期睾酮水平升高。这种激素有助于男孩发展肌肉力量、体毛以及产生精子。在 30 岁至 40 岁之间的某个时间点，男性的睾酮水平自然开始下降。估计有所不同，但睾酮水平低下被认为影响了全球 10-40% 的老年男性（外部）——这被认为影响到美国约 1300 万男性。这些男性可能会经历情绪低落、性欲下降和肌肉质量损失。血液测试可以检查睾酮水平去年领导美国食品和药物管理局 (FDA) 军队睾酮筛查和利用专家小组的 Mohit Khera 博士告诉 BBC，所有 30 岁以上的男性都应该接受筛查，因为它是一个人当前和未来整体健康状况的首要标志。“这里的关键是，许多年轻人的睾酮水平较低，这使他们在肌肉质量和能量方面处于劣势，如果你在战斗中，这可能会成为一个问题。”贝勒医学院泌尿科教授赫拉说。但他补充道：“你必须小心，不要只给某人注射睾酮，除非他们确实有某种症状。”他解释说，激素替代疗法的好处是增加肌肉质量，减少脂肪沉积，并降低患抑郁症的风险。凯拉补充说，随着时间的推移，它还可以帮助提高骨矿物质密度。不过，他警告说，这并不适合所有人。“如果年轻人服用睾酮，可能会使他们不育，”凯拉说。 “你必须小心。如果有人处于生育年龄，你需要确保他们明白这会让你不育。”他指出，理论上这也可能导致心血管风险增加。在美国卫生部长小罗伯特·F·肯尼迪和其他政府官员试图消除医疗专业人员开出男性睾酮处方的障碍之后，赫格斯宣布了这一消息。 FDA 上个月要求删除睾酮替代疗法产品标签上的安全性和有效性语言，并提议放宽睾酮产品的处方限制。米歇尔·罗伯茨 (Michelle Roberts) 的补充报告

articles.title: SpaceX share price drops below stock market debut
articles.summary: Share trading in Elon Musk’s rocket company has been volatile since it went public a month ago.
articles.titleZh: SpaceX 股价跌破上市首日价
articles.summaryZh: 埃隆·马斯克的火箭公司自一个月前上市以来，其股票交易一直波动较大。

contentParagraphs.en: SpaceX's share price has dropped below its stock market debut just over a month ago, falling sharply from a post-float peak.The price for a single share in Elon Musk's rocket, satellite and artificial intelligence (AI) company fell to $132.62 (£98.24) on Wednesday, below its initial listing of $135 in June.SpaceX's initial public offering (IPO) made Musk the world's first trillionaire. Compared to its on-the-day high so far, the stock price is now down 41%.If the price holds, or falls further, it will mean that those who purchased stock around the time of its flotation will stand to lose money on their investment.Even amid a tumultuous few weeks for tech stocks, SpaceX has taken a particular hit.Compared to a 0.2% fall on the wider Nasdaq index, where SpaceX's shares are listed, the company's stock price fell more than 2% on Wednesday.SpaceX stock has been volatile since it began trading on the public stock market a little over one month ago.After an initial investor frenzy that saw the company valued at more than Amazon and Microsoft, the price of its shares has drifted downward.Initially, SpaceX was treated by investors as the first chance they had to invest in an AI company, according to what financial market analysts and experts recently told the BBC, Earlier this year, SpaceX acquired Musk's AI start-up xAI, recently renamed SpaceXAI, marking it's first foray into an AI-focused business.XAI is best known for the controversial chatbot Grok, but through that acquisition, SpaceX now leases data centre capacity to other tech companies.The company's main business is the manufacture and launch of rockets and telecommunications satellites called Starlink.When Starlink said it was cutting prices in the Memphis, Tennessee area amid local concerns over a massive data centre project, SpaceX shares fell by 8%.Steve Sosnick, chief market analyst at Interactive Brokers, told Reuters: "There hasn't been anything that lately to remind people of some of the catalysts for why they bought SpaceX."SpaceX is expected to release in August its first public earnings report.Sosnick added: "The fact that a stock has fallen a couple of dollars below its IPO price in itself is not a tragedy, but SpaceX is heavily watched and has an important role in investor psyche."SpaceX did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
contentParagraphs.zh: SpaceX 的股价已跌破一个多月前的首次公开募股，从上市后的峰值大幅下跌。埃隆·马斯克旗下的火箭、卫星和人工智能 (AI) 公司的每股价格周三跌至 132.62 美元（合 98.24 英镑），低于 6 月份首次上市的 135 美元。SpaceX 的首次公开募股 (IPO) 让马斯克成为世界上第一位万亿富翁。与迄今为止的当日高点相比，该股价目前已下跌 41%。如果价格保持不变，或进一步下跌，这将意味着那些在其上市前后购买股票的人将在投资上亏损。即使在科技股动荡的几周内，SpaceX 也受到了特别的打击。与 SpaceX 股票上市的纳斯达克指数下跌 0.2% 相比，该公司股价跌幅超过周三，SpaceX 的股价上涨了 2%。自一个多月前在公开股票市场开始交易以来，SpaceX 的股票一直在波动。在最初的投资者狂热看到该公司的估值超过亚马逊和微软之后，其股价逐渐下跌。据金融市场分析师和专家最近向 BBC 透露，最初，SpaceX 被投资者视为他们投资人工智能公司的第一个机会，今年早些时候，SpaceX 收购了马斯克的人工智能初创公司 xAI，最近更名为 SpaceXAI， XAI 以备受争议的聊天机器人 Grok 闻名，但通过这次收购，SpaceX 现在将数据中心容量出租给其他科技公司。该公司的主要业务是制造和发射名为 Starlink 的火箭和电信卫星。当 Starlink 表示，由于当地对大型数据中心项目的担忧，它将在田纳西州孟菲斯地区降价时，SpaceX 股价下跌了 8%。 Interactive 首席市场分析师 Steve Sosnick经纪人告诉路透社：“最近没有任何事情提醒人们购买 SpaceX 的一些催化剂。” SpaceX 预计将在 8 月发布其第一份公开收益报告。索斯尼克补充道：“股票比 IPO 价格下跌几美元的事实本身并不是悲剧，但 SpaceX 受到密切关注，并且在投资者心理中发挥着重要作用。” SpaceX 没有立即回应置评请求。

articles.title: China's economy grows 4.3% in Q2, slowest since late 2022
articles.summary: Lagging consumer spending and business investment offset the boost from strong exports thanks partly to the boom in artificial intelligence.
articles.titleZh: 中国经济第二季度增长4.3%，为2022年底以来最低
articles.summaryZh: 落后的消费者支出和商业投资抵消了强劲出口的推动，部分原因是人工智能的繁荣。

contentParagraphs.en: Heavy equipment and cars are prepared for shipment by rail in Yantai, eastern China's Shandong province, on June 20, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: 2026 年 6 月 20 日，中国东部山东省烟台，重型设备和汽车准备通过铁路运输。

contentParagraphs.en: HONG KONG — China's economy slowed sharply to a 4.3% annualized pace of growth in the April-June quarter, the government said Wednesday, the weakest in over three years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 香港——政府周三表示，中国经济在 4 月至 6 月这个季度的年化增长率大幅放缓至 4.3%，为三年多来的最低水平。

contentParagraphs.en: The official data fell short of forecasts and was far below the economy's strong 5% pace of growth in January-March, despite a surge in exports driven partly by the boom in artificial intelligence, and by robust global demand for Chinese electric vehicles.
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管人工智能的蓬勃发展以及全球对中国电动汽车的强劲需求推动了出口激增，但官方数据仍低于预期，并远低于1月至3月经济5%的强劲增长速度。

contentParagraphs.en: China has largely shrugged off wider economic impacts from the Iran war as soaring energy prices pushed up global inflation. Exports rose 17.6% in the first half of the year from a year earlier, and 27% in June, according to customs data.
contentParagraphs.zh: 随着能源价格飙升推高全球通胀，中国基本上摆脱了伊朗战争带来的更广泛的经济影响。海关数据显示，上半年出口同比增长17.6%，6月份出口同比增长27%。

contentParagraphs.en: But domestic spending and investment have lagged, limiting the boost from export manufacturing for an economy that has struggled to regain momentum since parts of China were locked down during the COVID-19 pandemic.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但国内支出和投资却滞后，限制了出口制造业的提振，自新冠肺炎 (COVID-19) 大流行期间中国部分地区被封锁以来，中国经济一直难以恢复增长势头。

contentParagraphs.en: "This was the slowest growth in any quarter since the lockdown-impacted fourth quarter of 2022," said Lynn Song, chief economist for Greater China at ING Bank in a note.
contentParagraphs.zh: ING 银行大中华区首席经济学家 Lynn Song 在一份报告中表示：“这是自 2022 年第四季度受封锁影响以来最慢的增长。”

contentParagraphs.en: Some economists say China's economy is becoming increasingly unbalanced as heavy state support and private investments pour into frontier technologies like AI, computer chips and robotics while other areas such as lower-value manufacturing and jobs creating services industries languish.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一些经济学家表示，随着国家大力支持和私人投资涌入人工智能、计算机芯片和机器人等前沿技术，而低价值制造业和创造就业机会的服务业等其他领域却陷入困境，中国经济正变得越来越不平衡。

contentParagraphs.en: Exports of high-tech products such as electric vehicles, computer chips and other electronic equipment have risen sharply, helped by hefty government support since China's leaders have made development of advanced technologies a top priority.
contentParagraphs.zh: 由于中国领导人将发展先进技术作为首要任务，在政府的大力支持下，电动汽车、计算机芯片和其他电子设备等高科技产品的出口大幅增长。

contentParagraphs.en: China ran a record $1.2 trillion global trade surplus last year, drawing complaints from policymakers in other countries over their trade imbalances with the world's second-largest economy. Many have pointed to those heavy state subsidies, which they say contribute to an oversupply of manufactured goods that end up being exported overseas. Industrial output by value rose 5.4% in the first half of the year from a year earlier.
contentParagraphs.zh: 去年，中国的全球贸易顺差达到创纪录的1.2万亿美元，引起了其他国家政策制定者对其与世界第二大经济体的贸易不平衡的抱怨。许多人指出了这些巨额的国家补贴，他们说这导致了最终出口到海外的制成品供应过剩。上半年工业总产值同比增长5.4%。

contentParagraphs.en: As is true in many countries, the expansion of AI and robotics has also raised worries at home over whether businesses will create enough jobs to sustain growth in the longer term.
contentParagraphs.zh: 与许多国家一样，人工智能和机器人技术的扩张也引发了国内企业对企业能否创造足够就业机会以维持长期增长的担忧。

contentParagraphs.en: Chinese families have cut back on big purchases, their appetite for spending constrained by a prolonged property slump and uncertainties over jobs and wages.
contentParagraphs.zh: 中国家庭减少了大宗采购，他们的消费意愿因房地产长期低迷以及就业和工资的不确定性而受到限制。

contentParagraphs.en: As China remains reliant on its exports to sustain overall growth, "China's growth model has become increasingly imbalanced," said Eswar Prasad, a professor of economics and trade policy at Cornell University. Substantially increasing domestic demand will be tough as confidence remains weak, he added.
contentParagraphs.zh: 康奈尔大学经济与贸易政策教授埃斯瓦尔·普拉萨德表示，由于中国仍然依赖出口来维持总体增长，“中国的增长模式变得越来越不平衡”。他补充说，由于信心依然疲弱，内需大幅增长将困难重重。

contentParagraphs.en: Mao Shengyong, deputy head of China's National Bureau of Statistics, told reporters that given the increasingly unstable and uncertain global situation, the imbalance between strong supply and weak demand "remains acute" at home.
contentParagraphs.zh: 中国国家统计局副局长毛盛勇对记者表示，在国际形势不稳定不确定因素增多的情况下，国内供给旺盛与需求疲软的矛盾依然突出。

contentParagraphs.en: As China focuses on high-tech manufacturing and pursues "higher-quality economic growth," it will work to build a robust domestic market and offer support to keep employment stable, he said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他表示，中国聚焦高科技制造业，追求“更高质量的经济增长”，将努力建设强大的国内市场，为稳定就业提供支持。

contentParagraphs.en: Highlighting weaker points in the economy, investment in fixed assets, such as factory equipment, fell 5.7% year-on-year in the first half of the year, while retail sales of consumer goods climbed a meager 1.3%. Housing prices continued to fall.
contentParagraphs.zh: 上半年，工厂设备等固定资产投资同比下降5.7%，消费品零售额仅增长1.3%，凸显了经济的弱势。房价继续下跌。

contentParagraphs.en: China's economy is going through a "significant transition," said Wei Li, Head of Multi-Asset Investments at BNP Paribas Securities (China).
contentParagraphs.zh: 法国巴黎证券（中国）多元资产投资主管李伟表示，中国经济正在经历“重大转型”。

contentParagraphs.en: For the whole of 2026, Chinese leaders have set a growth target of 4.5% to 5%, slower than last year's 5%. Overall economic growth for the first half of the year was at 4.7%, the data released Wednesday showed.
contentParagraphs.zh: 对于2026年全年，中国领导人设定了4.5%至5%的增长目标，低于去年的5%。周三公布的数据显示，上半年整体经济增长4.7%。

contentParagraphs.en: The International Monetary Fund recently raised its forecast for China's annual growth by 0.2 percentage point to 4.6%. It expects China's economy to expand just 4.1% in 2027.
contentParagraphs.zh: 国际货币基金组织近期将中国全年经济增长预期上调0.2个百分点至4.6%。预计2027年中国经济仅增长4.1%。

articles.title: Scots urged to 'stay vigilant' as wildfire risk very high
articles.summary: The Scottish Fire and Rescue Service has issued a warning for central Highlands, southern and eastern Scotland from Wednesday.
articles.titleZh: 苏格兰人敦促“保持警惕”，因为野火风险非常高
articles.summaryZh: 苏格兰消防和救援局从周三开始向苏格兰中部高地、南部和东部发出警告。

contentParagraphs.en: Major incidents were declared in Conwy, north Wales, and in Glossop, Derbyshire, on Sunday, with firefighters still tackling the flames on Tuesday.Wildfires over the weekend also affected Hampshire, Durham, West Sussex, East Sussex, Devon and Somerset.The Scottish fire service's wildfire tactical adviser, Kevin Dingwall, said that many people will be outdoors enjoying the weather this week and urged them to take "simple steps" to prevent fires from starting.He said: "Hot, dry conditions increase the risk of wildfire across Scotland. Even if a warning is not in place where you live, we ask everyone to stay vigilant."Wildfires can destroy property, landscapes and wildlife. That's why it's so important that everyone takes extra care during these conditions."Avoid lighting outdoor fires or barbecues in affected areas, and remember that a single spark can cause significant damage. There is no such thing as a harmless fire."You can help by sharing wildfire warnings with your family, friends, and any visitors."
contentParagraphs.zh: QUERY LENGTH LIMIT EXCEEDED. MAX ALLOWED QUERY : 500 CHARS

articles.title: FIFA World Cup final: Date, time, halftime show, prediction and Trump
articles.summary: From Donald Trump's attendance to the match winner, the short guide before football’s global showpiece in New Jersey.
articles.titleZh: FIFA 世界杯决赛：日期、时间、半场表演、预测和特朗普
articles.summaryZh: 从唐纳德·特朗普的出席到比赛的获胜者，新泽西州全球足球盛事之前的简短指南。

contentParagraphs.en: From Donald Trump’s attendance to the match winner, the short guide before football’s global showpiece in New Jersey.The FIFA World Cup 2026 final will take place on Sunday, pitting Europe’s top-ranked team, Spain, against South America’s No 1 outfit, Argentina.Spain will look to add a second world title to their collection, while Argentina want to collect a fourth trophy.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Tuchel defends tactics after Argentina send England packing from World Cuplist 2 of 4Celebrations erupt in Argentina’s capital after World Cup semifinal winlist 3 of 4Argentina’s Falklands banner sparks controversy at World Cuplist 4 of 4VIDEO: Argentina rallies past England to reach FIFA World Cup finalend of listHere is everything to know about the title clash:When is the 2026 World Cup final?The World Cup final between Spain and Argentina will be held on Sunday, July 19.When did Spain and Argentina last face off?Spain and Argentina last played each other in an international friendly on March 27, 2018, at the Wanda Metropolitano stadium in Madrid. Spain won the match 6-1.What time does the World Cup final kick off?The match starts at 3pm local time in East Rutherford, New Jersey, which is 19:00 GMT. Where is the final being held?The World Cup final will be played at MetLife Stadium, home to two National Football League (NFL) teams: the New York Giants and the New York Jets. For the World Cup, the venue has temporarily been rebadged as New York-New Jersey Stadium.This huge multipurpose stadium holds up to 82,500 spectators for football fixtures. The venue also hosted the 2025 FIFA Club World Cup final and both semifinals.An aerial view showing MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey [Seth Wenig/AP Photo]Will US President Donald Trump attend the final?FIFA has already confirmed Donald Trump’s attendance at the 2026 World Cup final.The US president is expected to present the trophy to the winning side.Will there be a halftime show?For the first time in the event’s history, the World Cup final will have a Super Bowl-style halftime show.Entertainment stars such as Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira, BTS, Burna Boy, Gustavo Dudamel and the PS22 Chorus featuring Coldplay will perform.The performance itself is expected to run for about 11 minutes, with the halftime break potentially extended to up to 30 minutes. Separately, the final kickoff will also be preceded by a closing ceremony starting 90 minutes earlier. Entertainers such as Tom Cruise, Robbie Williams and Nicole Scherzinger are expected to feature.What is the prediction for Spain vs Argentina?Opta’s supercomputer has calculated a 45.1 percent probability of Spain winning the final in regulation time, while Argentina are assessed a 29.4 percent chance of victory.The probability of going to extra time – or potentially penalties – is 25.4 percent.Where is the 2030 World Cup taking place?The next tournament will take place across Morocco, Spain and Portugal. A venue city for the final has yet to be established, with Madrid the frontrunner.As part of the tournament’s 100th-anniversary celebrations, a centenary match will be played in Uruguay – which hosted the first tournament in 1930 – Argentina and Paraguay.
contentParagraphs.zh: 从唐纳德·特朗普的出席到比赛获胜者，这是新泽西州全球足球盛事之前的简短指南。2026 年国际足联世界杯决赛将于周日举行，欧洲排名第一的球队西班牙将对阵南美第一的球队阿根廷。西班牙将寻求在他们的收藏中再添第二个世界冠军，而阿根廷则希望获得第四个奖杯。推荐故事列表 4 项列表 1 of 4 图切尔在阿根廷将英格兰队从世界赛中淘汰后捍卫战术Cuplist 2 of 4 世界杯半决赛胜利后，阿根廷首都爆发庆祝活动 list 3 of 4 阿根廷的福克兰群岛旗帜在世界杯上引发争议 4 of 4 视频：阿根廷击败英格兰进入 FIFA 世界杯决赛名单结束以下是有关冠军争夺战的所有信息：2026 年世界杯决赛何时举行？西班牙和阿根廷之间的世界杯决赛将于 7 月 19 日星期日举行。西班牙和阿根廷上次交锋是什么时候西班牙和阿根廷上一次交手是在2018年3月27日在马德里万达大都会球场举行的国际友谊赛中。西班牙以 6-1 获胜。世界杯决赛几点开始？比赛于新泽西州东卢瑟福当地时间下午 3 点开始，即格林尼治标准时间 19:00。决赛在哪里举行？ 世界杯决赛将在大都会人寿体育场举行，这里是两支国家橄榄球联盟 (NFL) 球队：纽约巨人队和纽约喷气机队的主场。为了举办世界杯，该场馆已暂时重新命名为纽约-新泽西体育场。这座巨大的多功能体育场可容纳 82,500 名观众观看足球比赛。该场馆还举办了2025年国际足联俱乐部世界杯决赛和两场半决赛。新泽西州东卢瑟福大都会人寿体育场鸟瞰图[Seth Wenig/美联社照片]美国总统唐纳德·特朗普会出席决赛吗？国际足联已经确认唐纳德·特朗普将出席2026年世界杯决赛。美国总统预计将向获胜方颁发奖杯。是否会有中场表演？世界杯决赛在该赛事历史上首次将有一场超级碗式的中场表演。麦当娜、贾斯汀·比伯、夏奇拉、BTS、Burna Boy、古斯塔沃·杜达梅尔等娱乐明星以及酷玩乐队的 PS22 合唱团都将表演。表演本身预计持续约 11 分钟，中场休息可能会延长至 30 分钟。另外，最终开球前还将提前 90 分钟举行闭幕式。汤姆·克鲁斯 (Tom Cruise)、罗比·威廉姆斯 (Robbie Williams) 和妮可·斯彻金格 (Nicole Scherzinger) 等艺人预计将亮相。西班牙对阵阿根廷的预测是什么？Opta 的超级计算机计算出，西班牙在常规时间内赢得决赛的概率为 45.1%，而阿根廷的获胜概率为 29.4%。进入加时赛或潜在处罚的概率为 25.4%。2030 年世界杯在哪里举行？下一场比赛将在摩洛哥、西班牙和摩洛哥举行葡萄牙。决赛的举办城市尚未确定，其中马德里是领先者。 作为世界杯 100 周年庆祝活动的一部分，一场百年纪念赛将在乌拉圭举行，乌拉圭曾于 1930 年主办了首届世界杯，对手是阿根廷和巴拉圭。

articles.title: George Lucas likens AI sceptics to luddites clinging to horses and carts
articles.summary:
Star Wars director calls AI technology ‘the future’ of film-making and says ‘there’s nothing you can do about it’
The Star Wars director, George Lucas, has added his voice to the growing chorus of film-makers receptive to the rising use of AI tools in moviemaking.
Speaking in an
articles.titleZh: 乔治·卢卡斯将人工智能怀疑论者比作坚持马车的勒德分子
articles.summaryZh:
《星球大战》导演称人工智能技术是电影制作的“未来”，并表示“对此你无能为力”
越来越多的电影制作人接受人工智能工具在电影制作中的使用，《星球大战》导演乔治·卢卡斯也加入了这一行列。
讲话中

contentParagraphs.en: The Star Wars director, George Lucas, has added his voice to the growing chorus of film-makers receptive to the rising use of AI tools in moviemaking.Speaking in an interview with A Rabbit’s Foot, Lucas, 82, said: “Artificial intelligence means it’s much easier for us to make movies.”Resistance to the technology, he said, was “very much like sitting here saying: ‘Well, I believe the horse and the buggy is really where it’s at. These cars, they break down, they need gas, there’s all kinds of problems with them and pretty soon they’ll be making them into tanks, and then they’ll be killing people. It’s terrible.’”Adoption of such tools, continued Lucas, was an inevitability. “There’s nothing you can do about it. That’s progress, it’s the future.”Lucas is not the only director of a Star Wars film to have come out in favour of AI. The British film-maker Gareth Edwards, who made Rogue One: A Star Wars Story and most recently Jurassic World Rebirth said generative AI “is a fucking genius at helping you”.However, there are high-profile holdouts, including the director of The Odyssey, Christopher Nolan, who recently said: “I’ve never seen a technology that’s been so successfully adopted by Wall Street and by investors … that the public has so thoroughly rejected. Young people, in particular, they coined this term ‘AI slop’ … There’s a sort of disdain for things AI.”Meanwhile, Steven Soderbergh, whose documentary John Lennon: The Last Interview includes AI-generated sequences, has expressed ambivalence. “I don’t think it’s the solution to everything, and I don’t think it’s the death of everything. We are in the very early stages. Five years from now, we all may be going: ‘That was a fun phase.’”skip past newsletter promotionafter newsletter promotionLucas also told the website his thoughts on the industry’s use of audience testing and focus groups. “I don’t like focus groups,” he said. “The audience doesn’t know what they want to see. If they don’t like a character, that’s interesting, and as a film-maker I want to find out why. But when the studios hear that, they take the wrong message. They let the audience actually make the movie … Now, it’s all about what the fans think. That isn’t how you make the movie.”
contentParagraphs.zh: 《星球大战》导演乔治·卢卡斯 (George Lucas) 也加入了越来越多的电影制作人的行列，接受人工智能工具在电影制作中的日益使用。82 岁的卢卡斯在接受《兔脚》采访时表示：“人工智能意味着我们制作电影变得更加容易。”他说，对这项技术的抵制“就像坐在这里说：‘好吧，我相信马和马车真的就在它该在的地方’”这些汽车会发生故障，需要汽油，存在各种各样的问题，很快他们就会将它们制成坦克，然后它们就会杀人，这太可怕了。”卢卡斯继续说道，采用此类工具是不可避免的。 “你对此无能为力。这就是进步，这就是未来。” 卢卡斯并不是唯一一个公开支持人工智能的星球大战电影导演。英国电影制片人加雷斯·爱德华兹（Gareth Edwards）曾制作过《侠盗一号：星球大战外传》和最近的《侏罗纪世界重生》，他说生成式人工智能“真是个能帮助你的天才”。然而，也有一些高调的人持反对态度，其中包括《奥德赛》的导演克里斯托弗·诺兰，他最近表示：“我从未见过一项技术能够如此成功地被华尔街和投资者采用……而公众却如此彻底拒绝。尤其是年轻人，他们创造了这个术语。” ‘AI slop’……这是对人工智能的一种蔑视。”与此同时，史蒂文·索德伯格（Steven Soderbergh）表达了矛盾的情绪，他的纪录片《约翰·列侬：最后的采访》中包含了人工智能生成的序列。 “我不认为这是解决所有问题的办法，也不认为这是一切的消亡。我们正处于非常早期的阶段。五年后，我们都可能会说：‘那是一个有趣的阶段。’” “我不喜欢焦点小组，”他说。 “观众不知道他们想看什么。如果他们不喜欢某个角色，那很有趣，作为一名电影制作人，我想找出原因。但当制片厂听到这个消息时，他们接受了错误的信息。他们让观众真正制作电影……现在，一切都取决于粉丝的想法。这不是你制作电影的方式。”

articles.title: Thames Water returns to profit after raising bills
articles.summary: But the firm says it only has enough debt funding to keep the business going until the end of 2026.
articles.titleZh: 泰晤士水务公司在提高账单后恢复盈利
articles.summaryZh: 但该公司表示，其债务融资仅够维持业务运营到 2026 年底。

contentParagraphs.en: Thames Water has returned to a full-year profit after hiking its customers bills by 40% last year.The UK's largest water company reported post-tax income of £113m for the 12 months to the end of March, swinging from a £1.51bn post-tax loss the previous year.However, the firm's net debt also swelled to £18.5bn from £16.8bn as it said it only had enough debt funding to keep the business going until the end of 2026.The publication of Thames Water's results comes after the government rejected a proposed rescue deal for the business in June, pushing the firm one step closer to nationalisation.Under the terms of the deal, Thames' lenders wanted leniency from future pollution fines in return for writing off £9.4bn of its debt pile and investing new money.Thames Water said on Wednesday pollution incidents had fallen by 18%. It hit just over half of its performance targets.The firm said it was below target when it came to customer complaints, which jumped by 77% to 122,798 in a year. Bill complaints made up over three quarters of the total and doubled compared to the previous year.The results also reveal how money from its customer bills is not enough to fund the massive upgrades needed to improve its aging and historically underinvested infrastructure, which means it needs to borrow more money.The firm said debt had risen as it "continued to fund the business through... debt and internally generated cash flows".He told Reuters on Wednesday that its lenders "want to see what the new Burnham government thinks before providing more funding".Labour MP Andy Burnham is expected to become Prime Minister on Monday. He has previously called for Thames Water to be nationalised.Dr Heather Smith, a senior lecturer in water governance at Cranfield University, said a "possible outcome" for Thames Water was "a special administration regime, which is sort of a temporary public control"."That's not the same as full nationalisation," she added, saying it could be used if the business goes bust "but just to get it to a new buyer"."Long-term nationalisation, I think, would be a struggle because I think the costs of what we need for infrastructure upgrades are such that it would be a real struggle for the public purse to handle – more so than the private sector."Elsewhere in the financial results, Thames Water reported that Weston's pay had risen by £128,000 to £1.163m from £1.035m.Weston did not receive a bonus, but the firm paid out £4.1m in bonuses to other directors, up from £2.8m the previous year.Environment Secretary Emma Reynolds said: "It's outrageous that one of the worst-performing water companies is handing out bonuses and inflation-busting pay rises to its executives.""It flies in the face of basic fairness, and the British public are right to be furious. We've banned bonuses for polluting water bosses and will be taking action to prevent bonuses by any other name."Additional reporting by Simon Jack

articles.title: Cancer disparities researchers say federal funding changes have disrupted their work
articles.summary: In a survey, 93% of cancer researchers who study disparities said federal policy changes have affected them. Funding is harder to come by and they worry it's slowing progress in their field.
articles.titleZh: 癌症差异研究人员表示，联邦资助的变化扰乱了他们的工作
articles.summaryZh: 在一项调查中，93% 研究差异的癌症研究人员表示，联邦政策的变化对他们产生了影响。资金越来越难获得，他们担心这会减缓他们领域的进展。

contentParagraphs.en: Cancer disparities cost lives and researchers push to understand what is behind these gaps. Federal funding for such work has slowed since last year.
contentParagraphs.zh: 癌症差异会导致生命损失，研究人员正在努力了解这些差距背后的原因。自去年以来，联邦政府对此类工作的资助已经放缓。

contentParagraphs.en: Andrew Brookes/Image Source/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Andrew Brookes/图片来源/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: Andrew Brookes/Image Source/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: Andrew Brookes/图片来源/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: Why are some people more likely to get cancer, and to die from it than others? Rural Americans, for instance, are 18% more likely to die from cancer overall, and Black women are 35% more likely to die from breast cancer than white women. That's according to a recent report from the American Association for Cancer Research, or AACR.
contentParagraphs.zh: 为什么有些人比其他人更容易患癌症并死于癌症？例如，美国农村地区总体上死于癌症的可能性高出18% ，黑人女性死于乳腺癌的可能性比白人女性高出35%。这是根据美国癌症研究协会（ AACR ）最近的一份报告。

contentParagraphs.en: Cancer disparity researchers study these gaps and how to close them. Their work has contributed to reductions in many disparities.
contentParagraphs.zh: 癌症差异研究人员研究这些差距以及如何缩小它们。他们的工作有助于减少许多差异。

contentParagraphs.en: But the AACR report found that federal policy changes have affected about 93% of surveyed researchers in this field.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但AACR报告发现，联邦政策的变化影响了该领域约93%的受访研究人员。

contentParagraphs.en: "Many medical trials were stopped in the middle — meaning those patients suddenly didn't receive the treatments they were getting, because the funding stopped," says Mariana Stern, professor of preventive medicine and urology at the Keck School of Medicine of USC and chair of the report committee.
contentParagraphs.zh: 南加州大学凯克医学院（ Keck School of Medicine of USC ）预防医学和泌尿学教授、报告委员会主席玛丽安娜·斯特恩（ Mariana Stern ）说： “许多医学试验在中间停止，这意味着这些患者突然没有接受他们正在接受的治疗，因为资金停止了。”

contentParagraphs.en: The report draws on a survey of 122 researchers including professors, scientists and students. Seventy-eight percent said they've been unable to apply for funding, and 59% said ongoing research projects were disrupted. And 59% of respondents said the funding that was lost came from the National Institutes of Health, or the NIH.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该报告借鉴了对122名研究人员的调查，其中包括教授、科学家和学生。78%的受访者表示他们无法申请资金， 59%的受访者表示正在进行的研究项目受到干扰。59%的受访者表示，损失的资金来自美国国立卫生研究院（ NIH ）。

contentParagraphs.en: The report also points to data published in JAMA Oncology in November showing that in roughly the first half of 2025, the Trump administration canceled 181 grants from the National Cancer Institute, or NCI, a division of the NIH. The grants totaled more than $317 million and many studied disparities.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该报告还指出， 11月发表在JAMA Oncology上的数据显示，大约在2025年上半年，特朗普政府取消了国家癌症研究所（ NCI ）的181笔拨款。补助金总额超过3.17亿美元，许多人研究了差距。

contentParagraphs.en: All told, thousands of grants across NIH were terminated in 2025, according to a non-profit called Grant Witness which tracks terminations and other changes to grant funding for scientific agencies. These funding cuts followed an executive order in January 2025 calling to end "radical" and "wasteful" DEI research.
contentParagraphs.zh: 总而言之，根据一个名为Grant Witness的非营利组织的说法，美国国立卫生研究院的数千笔赠款在2025年被终止，该组织追踪终止和其他变化，以资助科学机构。在削减资金之前， 2025年1月发布了一项行政命令，要求结束“激进”和“浪费”的DEI研究。

contentParagraphs.en: Heather Pierce, senior director for science policy at the Association of American Medical Colleges, AAMC, described the funding disruption as a mass termination "for simply not moving forward priorities of a new administration." She says that under prior administrations "termination was reserved as a very extreme remedy," and that the scale was unprecedented.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国医学院协会（ AAMC ）科学政策高级主任希瑟·皮尔斯（ Heather Pierce ）将资金中断描述为大规模终止， “因为根本没有推进新政府的优先事项。“她说，在前几届政府中， ”解雇被保留为一种非常极端的补救措施，“而且规模是前所未有的。

contentParagraphs.en: While many cancelled NIH grants were restored after several court cases fighting the change, there have been other terminations since, reported by the Department of Health and Human Services, the agency overseeing the NIH. And, fewer new grants from the National Cancer Institute and the National Institute of Minority Health and Health Disparities have been awarded this year than they have at this time in previous years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 虽然在几起反对这一变化的法庭案件之后，许多被取消的NIH拨款已经恢复，但根据监督NIH的卫生和公共服务部的报告，此后还有其他终止。而且，今年国家癌症研究所和国家少数民族健康与健康差异研究所获得的新补助金少于往年同期。

contentParagraphs.en: There have been other disruptions with grant funding. Pierce says that there has been a "continued slowdown this year" in grant making and renewals.
contentParagraphs.zh: 赠款资金还有其他中断。皮尔斯说，拨款和续签“今年持续放缓”。

contentParagraphs.en: Some researchers in the field are alarmed about the funding slowdown — and are worried that as it hinders their ability to do their work, it will translate to poorer patient outcomes.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该领域的一些研究人员对资金增长放缓感到震惊，并担心这会阻碍他们完成工作的能力，从而导致更差的患者预后。

contentParagraphs.en: "If we take our foot off the pedal of disparities research, there will be more Americans — in rural communities and others not near [centers of excellence] — who will not do well," says Dr. Robert Winn, the cancer center director at the Fox Chase Cancer Center in Pennsylvania who was not an author of the 2026 report but whose work was discussed in it.
contentParagraphs.zh: 宾夕法尼亚州福克斯蔡斯癌症中心癌症中心主任罗伯特温恩博士说： “如果我们放弃差距研究的脚踏板，将会有更多的美国人—在农村社区和其他不靠近[卓越中心]的人-不会做得很好。”他不是2026年报告的作者，但其工作在其中得到了讨论。

contentParagraphs.en: In response to questions about cuts to cancer disparities funding, the NIH said in a statement: "NCI identifies cancer disparity research as a priority and recognizes that advancements in the field generate knowledge benefiting all patients across the cancer continuum, from prevention and early detection to treatment and survivorship."
contentParagraphs.zh: 在回答有关削减癌症差异资金的问题时，美国国立卫生研究院在一份声明中说： “NCI将癌症差异研究确定为优先事项，并认识到该领域的进步产生的知识使整个癌症连续体中的所有患者受益，从预防和早期发现到治疗和生存。”

contentParagraphs.en: "Completely unprecedented"
contentParagraphs.zh: “前所未有”

contentParagraphs.en: Scarlett Lin Gomez, a professor of epidemiology and biostatistics at University of California, San Francisco, says the Greater Bay Area Cancer Registry, which she leads, has gotten NCI funding for the last 53 years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 加州大学旧金山分校流行病学和生物统计学教授斯嘉丽·林·戈麦斯（ Scarlett Lin Gomez ）表示，她领导的大湾区癌症登记处在过去53年中获得了NCI的资助。

contentParagraphs.en: "We received a cut that was completely unprecedented in the years my registry has been funded through the NCI. We've not had a cut of this magnitude at all — by far," Gomez says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “在我的注册机构通过NCI获得资金的这些年中，我们获得了前所未有的削减。到目前为止，我们还没有得到如此规模的削减，”戈麦斯说。

contentParagraphs.en: Gomez runs a lab of approximately 50 people. Gomez says she had to let go of about seven full-time employees last year and expects she'll have to let go five or six more this year.
contentParagraphs.zh: Gomez经营着一个约50人的实验室。戈麦斯说，去年她不得不解雇大约七名全职员工，预计今年她将不得不再解雇五六名全职员工。

contentParagraphs.en: "It literally keeps me up at night — the impacts on individuals in my lab," Gomez says. "I'm hearing from colleagues over the past several months [that] everybody's looking at doing the same thing … It's scary to even think about what that's going to look like and what the impact is going to be."
contentParagraphs.zh: Gomez说： “它确实让我夜不能寐--这对我实验室中的个体产生了影响。” “在过去的几个月里，我从同事那里听到，每个人都在考虑做同样的事情……甚至想想那会是什么样子，会产生什么影响，这都很可怕。”

contentParagraphs.en: Winn concurs: "It's been disruptive. It's forced folks to be creative to keep things going."
contentParagraphs.zh: Winn同意： “这是破坏性的。它迫使人们发挥创造力，让事情继续下去。”

contentParagraphs.en: Stern, who researches cancer epidemiology, personally had to reframe some of her work to stay compliant and keep funding. In some cases, she simply reworded funding requests. In other cases, she had to change the focus of the project.
contentParagraphs.zh: 研究癌症流行病学的斯特恩个人不得不重新构建她的一些工作，以保持合规性和保持资金。在某些情况下，她只是重新措辞了资助申请。在其他情况下，她不得不改变项目的重点。

contentParagraphs.en: Stern had previously gotten federal grant funding for training and career development for students from racial and ethnic minorities to help them get into medical school or go into research. This is important, she says, to "overcome structural reasons minorities are underrepresented" in medicine.
contentParagraphs.zh: Stern此前曾获得联邦拨款，用于种族和少数民族学生的培训和职业发展，以帮助他们进入医学院或进入研究领域。她说，这对于“克服少数群体在医学领域代表性不足的结构性原因”非常重要。

contentParagraphs.en: "The data show that patients do better when they're treated by people who look like them, speak their language, and understand their culture — and research moves faster when teams are diverse and include members of the communities they're trying to understand," Stern says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 斯特恩说： “数据显示，当患者接受与他们相似的人，说他们的语言，了解他们的文化时，他们会做得更好—当团队多样化并包括他们试图理解的社区的成员时，研究会更快地进行。”

contentParagraphs.en: But, she says, the researchers had to change focus under Trump's executive orders: "We are no longer allowed to have programs that target exclusively racial and ethnic minorities."
contentParagraphs.zh: 但是，她说，根据特朗普的行政命令，研究人员不得不改变重点： “我们不再被允许拥有专门针对种族和少数民族的计划。”
